# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import paramiko
import sys

sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect(
    "5.188.18.103",
    username="root",
    password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi",
    timeout=25,
    allow_agent=False,
    look_for_keys=False,
)

script = r"""
set -e
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get install -y -qq build-essential libssl-dev liblzo2-dev liblz4-dev libpam0g-dev pkg-config libnl-genl-3-dev libcap-ng-dev autoconf automake libtool wget >/tmp/builddeps.log 2>&1
cd /tmp
rm -rf openvpn-2.7.5 openvpn-2.7.5.tar.gz
wget -q https://swupdate.openvpn.org/community/releases/openvpn-2.7.5.tar.gz -O openvpn-2.7.5.tar.gz || wget -q https://github.com/OpenVPN/openvpn/releases/download/v2.7.5/openvpn-2.7.5.tar.gz -O openvpn-2.7.5.tar.gz
tar xf openvpn-2.7.5.tar.gz
cd openvpn-2.7.5
./configure --prefix=/usr/local --enable-shared=no --disable-unit-tests 2>&1 | tail -20
make -j$(nproc) 2>&1 | tail -30
make install 2>&1 | tail -10
/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --version | head -4 || /usr/local/bin/openvpn --version | head -4
which -a openvpn
# Point systemd unit to new binary by drop-in
mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@server.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@server.service.d/override.conf <<'EOF'
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --status /run/openvpn-server/status-server.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --config server.conf
EOF
# if binary is in bin not sbin
if [ ! -x /usr/local/sbin/openvpn ] && [ -x /usr/local/bin/openvpn ]; then
  sed -i 's|/usr/local/sbin/openvpn|/usr/local/bin/openvpn|' /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@server.service.d/override.conf
fi
systemctl daemon-reload
# keep current server.conf (tls-auth)
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server
ss -ulnp | grep 1194
ps aux | grep '[o]penvpn'
nft flush chain ip nat POSTROUTING 2>/dev/null || true
nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "ens3" ip saddr 10.8.0.0/24 masquerade
journalctl -u openvpn-server@server -n 15 --no-pager
echo BUILD_OK
"""

sftp = c.open_sftp()
with sftp.file("/tmp/build_ovpn27.sh", "w") as f:
    f.write(script)
sftp.chmod("/tmp/build_ovpn27.sh", 0o700)
sftp.close()
print("building... this may take a few minutes")
_, o, e = c.exec_command("bash /tmp/build_ovpn27.sh", timeout=600)
print(o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
err = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
if err.strip():
    print("ERR", err[-4000:])
c.close()

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import paramiko, sys
sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect("5.188.18.103", username="root", password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi", timeout=25, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)
script = r"""
set -e
/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --version | head -3
# Fix unit override - use correct WorkingDirectory and binary
mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@server.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@server.service.d/override.conf <<'EOF'
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --status /run/openvpn-server/status-server.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --config server.conf
TimeoutStartSec=30
EOF
# Ensure config dir
ls -la /etc/openvpn/server/server.conf
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl reset-failed openvpn-server@server
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server || { journalctl -u openvpn-server@server -n 40 --no-pager; systemctl status openvpn-server@server --no-pager; exit 1; }
ss -ulnp | grep 1194
ps aux | grep '[o]penvpn'
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1
nft flush chain ip nat POSTROUTING 2>/dev/null || true
nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "ens3" ip saddr 10.8.0.0/24 masquerade
echo OK
"""
_, o, e = c.exec_command(script, timeout=60)
print(o.read().decode())
print(e.read().decode()[-2000:])
c.close()

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Fix systemd + temporarily disable redirect-gateway so remote access stays up while testing data channel."""
import pathlib
import re
import sys

import paramiko

sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")

c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect(
    "5.188.18.103",
    username="root",
    password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi",
    timeout=25,
    allow_agent=False,
    look_for_keys=False,
)

conf = r"""port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
user nobody
group nogroup
persist-key
persist-tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.0.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp.txt
# NO redirect-gateway yet — keep office/remote-access routing intact while testing tunnel
push "dhcp-option DNS 1.1.1.1"
push "dhcp-option DNS 8.8.8.8"
keepalive 10 120
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
tls-auth /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key 0
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
explicit-exit-notify 1
tun-mtu 1400
mssfix 1360
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn.log
verb 4
mute 10
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
"""

override = r"""[Service]
Type=simple
RemainAfterExit=no
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
TimeoutStartSec=0
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --status /run/openvpn-server/status-server.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --config server.conf
"""

script = r"""
set -e
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server.conf <<'EOF'
""" + conf + """EOF
mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@server.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@server.service.d/override.conf <<'EOF'
""" + override + """EOF
systemctl daemon-reload
# soft restart: brief blip on 1194 only, does not touch client Windows routing of remote access
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server
sleep 2
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1
nft flush chain ip nat POSTROUTING 2>/dev/null || true
nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "ens3" ip saddr 10.8.0.0/24 masquerade
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server || true
ss -ulnp | grep 1194
ps aux | grep '[o]penvpn' | grep -v grep
# rebuild client ovpns (no redirect needed client-side; server won't push it)
EASY=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa
OUT=/root/openvpn-clients
TA=$(cat /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key)
CA=$(cat /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt)
for u in user1 user2; do
  CERT=$(awk '/BEGIN CERTIFICATE/,/END CERTIFICATE/' $EASY/pki/issued/$u.crt)
  KEY=$(cat $EASY/pki/private/$u.key)
  cat > "$OUT/$u.ovpn" <<EOF
client
dev tun
proto udp
remote 5.188.18.103 1194
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
verb 3
tun-mtu 1400
mssfix 1360
disable-dco
windows-driver tap-windows6
key-direction 1
<ca>
$CA
</ca>
<cert>
$CERT
</cert>
<key>
$KEY
</key>
<tls-auth>
$TA
</tls-auth>
EOF
done
echo CLIENTS_OK
"""

sftp = c.open_sftp()
with sftp.file("/tmp/safe_fix_ovpn.sh", "w") as f:
    f.write(script)
sftp.chmod("/tmp/safe_fix_ovpn.sh", 0o700)
_, o, e = c.exec_command("bash /tmp/safe_fix_ovpn.sh", timeout=60)
print(o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
err = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
if err.strip():
    print("ERR", err[-1500:])

desk = pathlib.Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")
gui = pathlib.Path(r"C:\Users\cherkasov.n\OpenVPN\config")
gui.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
for u in ("user1", "user2"):
    data = sftp.file(f"/root/openvpn-clients/{u}.ovpn").read().decode()
    for p in (desk / f"openvpn-bypass-{u}.ovpn", gui / f"openvpn-bypass-{u}.ovpn"):
        p.write_text(data, encoding="utf-8", newline="\n")
        print("saved", p)

sftp.close()
c.close()
print("DONE")

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import paramiko, sys
sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect("5.188.18.103", username="root", password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi", timeout=25, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)

# What epoch-related options exist?
_, o, _ = c.exec_command("/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --help 2>&1 | grep -iE 'epoch|compat|legacy|aeade'", timeout=15)
print("HELP:", o.read().decode())

conf = r"""port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
user nobody
group nogroup
persist-key
persist-tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.0.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp.txt
push "dhcp-option DNS 1.1.1.1"
push "dhcp-option DNS 8.8.8.8"
keepalive 10 120
compat-mode 2.6.0
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
tls-auth /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key 0
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
explicit-exit-notify 1
tun-mtu 1400
mssfix 1360
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn.log
verb 4
mute 10
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
"""
sftp = c.open_sftp()
with sftp.file("/etc/openvpn/server/server.conf", "w") as f:
    f.write(conf)
sftp.close()

_, o, e = c.exec_command(
    "systemctl restart openvpn-server@server; sleep 2; systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server; "
    "ss -ulnp | grep 1194; nft flush chain ip nat POSTROUTING; "
    "nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname ens3 ip saddr 10.8.0.0/24 masquerade; "
    "sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1",
    timeout=30,
)
print(o.read().decode())
print(e.read().decode()[-800:])
c.close()
print("SERVER_OK")

# Update configs: drop persist-tun so old TAP is not reused
foreach ($f in @(
  'C:\Users\cherkasov.n\OpenVPN\config\openvpn-bypass-user1.ovpn',
  'C:\Users\cherkasov.n\OpenVPN\config\openvpn-bypass-user2.ovpn',
  'E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop\openvpn-bypass-user1.ovpn',
  'E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop\openvpn-bypass-user2.ovpn'
)) {
  if (-not (Test-Path $f)) { continue }
  $t = Get-Content $f -Raw
  $t = $t -replace '(?m)^persist-tun\r?\n',''
  if ($t -notmatch 'windows-driver wintun') {
    $t = $t -replace 'disable-dco',"disable-dco`nwindows-driver wintun"
  } else {

  Write-Host "cfg $f"
}

echo Done. Check tray OpenVPN GUI. Ping test: ping 10.8.0.1
pause
'@

content.text: Да — OpenVPN оставлял DNS `1.1.1.1`/`8.8.8.8` и мёртвые маршруты `/1` на TAP. Из‑за этого ломается домен. Сейчас чищу это и убираю push DNS с сервера.

input.contents:
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Remove DNS push from OpenVPN server — AD/domain must use office DNS."""
import paramiko, sys
sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect("5.188.18.103", username="root", password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi", timeout=25, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)
conf = r"""port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
user nobody
group nogroup
persist-key
persist-tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.0.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp.txt
# Do NOT push public DNS — breaks AD/domain login on Windows
# Do NOT push redirect-gateway until data-channel proven safe for remote access
keepalive 10 120
compat-mode 2.6.0
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
tls-auth /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key 0
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
explicit-exit-notify 1
tun-mtu 1400
mssfix 1360
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn.log
verb 4
mute 10
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
"""
sftp = c.open_sftp()
with sftp.file("/etc/openvpn/server/server.conf", "w") as f:
    f.write(conf)
sftp.close()
_, o, e = c.exec_command(
    "systemctl restart openvpn-server@server; sleep 2; "
    "systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server; "
    "grep -E 'dhcp-option|redirect-gateway|DNS' /etc/openvpn/server/server.conf || echo 'NO_DNS_PUSH_OK'; "
    "ss -ulnp | grep 1194; "
    "nft flush chain ip nat POSTROUTING; nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname ens3 ip saddr 10.8.0.0/24 masquerade; "
    "sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1",
    timeout=40,
)
print(o.read().decode())
err = e.read().decode()
if err.strip():
    print("ERR", err[-800:])
c.close()

@echo off
chcp 65001 >nul
echo === Cleanup OpenVPN leftovers (DNS + dead routes) ===
echo Does NOT touch Radmin / Ethernet domain DNS 192.168.2.103-104
echo.

echo [1] Kill leftover openvpn client if any...
taskkill /IM openvpn.exe /F >nul 2>&1
taskkill /IM openvpn-gui.exe /F >nul 2>&1

echo [2] Remove dead full-tunnel routes via 10.8.0.1...
route delete 0.0.0.0 mask 128.0.0.0 10.8.0.1 >nul 2>&1
route delete 128.0.0.0 mask 128.0.0.0 10.8.0.1 >nul 2>&1
route delete 5.188.18.103 mask 255.255.255.255 192.168.2.1 >nul 2>&1

echo [3] Clear DNS 1.1.1.1/8.8.8.8 from OpenVPN TAP...
netsh interface ip set dnsservers "OpenVPN TAP-Windows6" dhcp >nul 2>&1
netsh interface ip delete dnsservers "OpenVPN TAP-Windows6" all >nul 2>&1
netsh interface ip set address "OpenVPN TAP-Windows6" dhcp >nul 2>&1

echo [4] Disable OpenVPN TAP/DCO adapters (leave Ethernet + Radmin)...
netsh interface set interface "OpenVPN TAP-Windows6" admin=disabled >nul 2>&1
netsh interface set interface "OpenVPN Connect DCO Adapter" admin=disabled >nul 2>&1
netsh interface set interface "OpenVPN Data Channel Offload" admin=disabled >nul 2>&1

echo [5] Flush DNS cache...
ipconfig /flushdns >nul

echo [6] Show DNS now:
powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-DnsClientServerAddress -AddressFamily IPv4 | ? ServerAddresses | ft InterfaceAlias,ServerAddresses -AutoSize"
powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-NetRoute -AddressFamily IPv4 | ? { $_.DestinationPrefix -match '0.0.0.0/1|128.0.0.0/1' } | ft DestinationPrefix,NextHop,InterfaceAlias"

echo.
echo Done. Domain DNS should be only on Ethernet: 192.168.2.103 / 192.168.2.104
pause

input.description: Remove server DNS push; try local cleanup

На Ethernet DNS уже правильные: `192.168.2.103`, `192.168.2.104`. Доменные контроллеры/MikroTik я не трогал — только этот ПК и push в OpenVPN. Если кто-то ещё подключал наши `.ovpn`, у них тоже мог остаться `1.1.1.1` — им тот же сброс или просто Disconnect + `ipconfig /flushdns`.

chcp 65001 >nul
echo === FIX network leftovers from OpenVPN ===

echo Radmin is not touched.
echo.

taskkill /IM openvpn.exe /F >nul 2>&1
taskkill /IM openvpn-gui.exe /F >nul 2>&1

echo [1] Dead VPN full-tunnel routes...

route delete 128.0.0.0 mask 128.0.0.0 10.8.0.1
route delete 5.188.18.103 mask 255.255.255.255 192.168.2.1

echo [2] Leftover OpenVPN LAN routes forced via gateway (break local servers)...
route delete 192.168.2.0 mask 255.255.255.0 192.168.2.1

input.contents:
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Hardening OpenVPN server for working data channel; no DNS/redirect yet."""
import pathlib
import re
import sys

import paramiko

sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")

c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect(
    "5.188.18.103",
    username="root",
    password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi",
    timeout=25,
    allow_agent=False,
    look_for_keys=False,
)

conf = r"""port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.0.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp.txt
keepalive 10 120
# Keep data-channel simple / compatible with Windows 2.7.5
compat-mode 2.6.0
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
tls-auth /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key 0
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
explicit-exit-notify 1
tun-mtu 1400
mssfix 1360
user nobody
group nogroup
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn.log
verb 4
mute 10
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
# NO dhcp-option DNS (breaks AD)
# NO redirect-gateway yet (enable after tunnel ping works)
"""

override = r"""[Service]
Type=simple
TimeoutStartSec=0
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --status /run/openvpn-server/status-server.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --config server.conf
"""

script = f"""
set -e
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server.conf <<'EOF'
{conf}
EOF
mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@server.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@server.service.d/override.conf <<'EOF'
{override}
EOF
# Ensure tls-auth key exists
test -f /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key || openvpn --genkey secret /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server
sleep 2
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1
sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter=2
sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.tun0.rp_filter=2 || true
nft flush chain ip nat POSTROUTING 2>/dev/null || true
nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "ens3" ip saddr 10.8.0.0/24 masquerade
nft flush chain ip filter FORWARD 2>/dev/null || true
nft add rule ip filter FORWARD iifname "tun0" oifname "ens3" accept
nft add rule ip filter FORWARD iifname "ens3" oifname "tun0" ct state related,established accept
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server
ss -ulnp | grep 1194
ip -4 addr show tun0
ping -c 2 -W 2 -I 10.8.0.1 1.1.1.1 | tail -5
# rebuild client profiles
EASY=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa
OUT=/root/openvpn-clients
mkdir -p "$OUT"
TA=$(cat /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key)
CA=$(cat /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt)
for u in user1 user2; do
  CERT=$(awk '/BEGIN CERTIFICATE/,/END CERTIFICATE/' "$EASY/pki/issued/$u.crt")
  KEY=$(cat "$EASY/pki/private/$u.key")
  cat > "$OUT/$u.ovpn" <<EOF
client
dev tun
proto udp
remote 5.188.18.103 1194
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
remote-cert-tls server
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
verb 3
tun-mtu 1400
mssfix 1360
disable-dco
windows-driver wintun
key-direction 1
<ca>
$CA
</ca>
<cert>
$CERT
</cert>
<key>
$KEY
</key>
<tls-auth>
$TA
</tls-auth>
EOF
done
echo READY
"""

sftp = c.open_sftp()
with sftp.file("/tmp/ovpn_ready.sh", "w") as f:
    f.write(script)
sftp.chmod("/tmp/ovpn_ready.sh", 0o700)
_, o, e = c.exec_command("bash /tmp/ovpn_ready.sh", timeout=60)
print(o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
err = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
if err.strip():
    print("ERR", err[-1500:])

desk = pathlib.Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")
gui = pathlib.Path(r"C:\Users\cherkasov.n\OpenVPN\config")
gui.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
for u in ("user1", "user2"):
    data = sftp.file(f"/root/openvpn-clients/{u}.ovpn").read().decode()
    for p in (desk / f"openvpn-bypass-{u}.ovpn", gui / f"openvpn-bypass-{u}.ovpn"):
        p.write_text(data, encoding="utf-8", newline="\n")
        print("saved", p, p.stat().st_size)

sftp.close()
c.close()
print("SERVER_DONE")

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import paramiko, sys
sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect("5.188.18.103", username="root", password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi", timeout=20, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)
cmd = r"""
set -e
cd /tmp/openvpn-2.7.5
grep -RIn --include='*.c' --include='*.h' -E 'aead_epoch|AEAD_EPOCH|disable_epoch|EPOCH|compat_mode_2_6' src/openvpn | head -80
echo ====
/usr/sbin/openvpn --version | head -1 || true
/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --version | head -1 || true
# Try running package 2.6 binary briefly to see if we can avoid aead-epoch
grep -n compat_mode src/openvpn/options.c | head -40
"""
_, o, e = c.exec_command(cmd, timeout=30)
print(o.read().decode())
print(e.read().decode()[-1000:])
c.close()

content.text: OpenVPN: сессия жива, но IP с Windows в туннель не попадает (байт на сервере не растёт). Поднимаю WireGuard точечно: сначала только `10.9.0.0/24`, без DNS и без ломки LAN.

input.contents:
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Setup clean WireGuard on VPS for working bypass; test subnet only first."""
import pathlib
import sys
import textwrap

import paramiko

sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")

c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect(
    "5.188.18.103",
    username="root",
    password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi",
    timeout=25,
    allow_agent=False,
    look_for_keys=False,
)

script = r"""
set -e
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get install -y -qq wireguard wireguard-tools >/tmp/wg_apt.log 2>&1 || true
mkdir -p /etc/wireguard /root/wg-clients

# Reuse or create server keys
if [ ! -f /etc/wireguard/server_private.key ]; then
  umask 077
  wg genkey | tee /etc/wireguard/server_private.key | wg pubkey > /etc/wireguard/server_public.key
fi
# Client keys
for u in user1 user2; do
  if [ ! -f /etc/wireguard/${u}_private.key ]; then
    wg genkey | tee /etc/wireguard/${u}_private.key | wg pubkey > /etc/wireguard/${u}_public.key
  fi
done

SP=$(cat /etc/wireguard/server_private.key)
SPUB=$(cat /etc/wireguard/server_public.key)
U1PRIV=$(cat /etc/wireguard/user1_private.key)
U1PUB=$(cat /etc/wireguard/user1_public.key)
U2PRIV=$(cat /etc/wireguard/user2_private.key)
U2PUB=$(cat /etc/wireguard/user2_public.key)

# Dedicated iface wg-bypass UDP 51820 (avoid old wg-ovpn mess if present)
cat > /etc/wireguard/wg-bypass.conf <<EOF
[Interface]
Address = 10.10.0.1/24
ListenPort = 51820
PrivateKey = $SP
PostUp = sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1; nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname ens3 ip saddr 10.10.0.0/24 masquerade 2>/dev/null || true
PostDown = true

[Peer]
# user1
PublicKey = $U1PUB
AllowedIPs = 10.10.0.2/32

[Peer]
# user2
PublicKey = $U2PUB
AllowedIPs = 10.10.0.3/32
EOF

# Client configs: START with tunnel-only (no full internet) for safe test
# Later we switch AllowedIPs to bypass internet while excluding LAN
mk_client() {
  local name="$1" priv="$2" ip="$3"
  # Phase A test config
  cat > /root/wg-clients/${name}-test.conf <<EOF
[Interface]
PrivateKey = $priv
Address = ${ip}/24
DNS = 192.168.2.103, 192.168.2.104

[Peer]
PublicKey = $SPUB
Endpoint = 5.188.18.103:51820
AllowedIPs = 10.10.0.0/24
PersistentKeepalive = 25
EOF
  # Phase B full bypass WITHOUT stealing LAN/DNS domain nets
  cat > /root/wg-clients/${name}-bypass.conf <<EOF
[Interface]
PrivateKey = $priv
Address = ${ip}/24
# Do not set DNS here — keep AD DNS on Ethernet

[Peer]
PublicKey = $SPUB
Endpoint = 5.188.18.103:51820
# Full tunnel split: all internet via WG, LAN stays local (not listed)
AllowedIPs = 0.0.0.0/1, 128.0.0.0/1
PersistentKeepalive = 25
EOF
}
mk_client user1 "$U1PRIV" 10.10.0.2
mk_client user2 "$U2PRIV" 10.10.0.3

# Open firewall UDP 51820
nft list ruleset | grep -q 'udp dport 51820' || nft add rule inet filter input udp dport 51820 accept 2>/dev/null || true
iptables -C INPUT -p udp --dport 51820 -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || iptables -I INPUT -p udp --dport 51820 -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || true

systemctl enable wg-quick@wg-bypass
systemctl restart wg-quick@wg-bypass
sleep 1

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Reuse wg-ovpn:51821; emit working client configs; ensure NAT."""
import pathlib
import sys

import paramiko

sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")

c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect(
    "5.188.18.103",
    username="root",
    password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi",
    timeout=25,
    allow_agent=False,
    look_for_keys=False,
)

script = r"""
set -e
# Disable failed unit
systemctl disable --now wg-quick@wg-bypass 2>/dev/null || true
rm -f /etc/wireguard/wg-bypass.conf

# Ensure keys for bypass clients
umask 077
mkdir -p /etc/wireguard /root/wg-clients
if [ ! -f /etc/wireguard/wg-ovpn.key ]; then
  # extract from running iface if possible
  wg show wg-ovpn private-key > /etc/wireguard/wg-ovpn.key 2>/dev/null || wg genkey > /etc/wireguard/wg-ovpn.key
fi
SP=$(cat /etc/wireguard/wg-ovpn.key)
SPUB=$(printf '%s' "$SP" | wg pubkey)
echo "$SPUB" > /etc/wireguard/wg-ovpn.pub

for u in user1 user2; do
  if [ ! -f /etc/wireguard/${u}_private.key ]; then
    wg genkey | tee /etc/wireguard/${u}_private.key | wg pubkey > /etc/wireguard/${u}_public.key
  fi
done
U1PRIV=$(cat /etc/wireguard/user1_private.key); U1PUB=$(cat /etc/wireguard/user1_public.key)
U2PRIV=$(cat /etc/wireguard/user2_private.key); U2PUB=$(cat /etc/wireguard/user2_public.key)

cat > /etc/wireguard/wg-ovpn.conf <<EOF
[Interface]
Address = 10.9.0.1/24
ListenPort = 51821
PrivateKey = $SP
PostUp = sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1; nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "ens3" ip saddr 10.9.0.0/24 masquerade 2>/dev/null || iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -s 10.9.0.0/24 -o ens3 -j MASQUERADE 2>/dev/null || true
PostDown = true

[Peer]
PublicKey = $U1PUB
AllowedIPs = 10.9.0.2/32

[Peer]
PublicKey = $U2PUB
AllowedIPs = 10.9.0.3/32
EOF

systemctl enable wg-quick@wg-ovpn
systemctl restart wg-quick@wg-ovpn
sleep 1
wg show wg-ovpn
ss -ulnp | grep 51821

# NAT
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1
nft list chain ip nat POSTROUTING 2>/dev/null | grep -q '10.9.0.0/24' || nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "ens3" ip saddr 10.9.0.0/24 masquerade
# FORWARD
nft list chain ip filter FORWARD 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'wg-ovpn' || {
  nft add rule ip filter FORWARD iifname "wg-ovpn" oifname "ens3" accept 2>/dev/null || true
  nft add rule ip filter FORWARD iifname "ens3" oifname "wg-ovpn" ct state related,established accept 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Client files
cat > /root/wg-clients/user1-test.conf <<EOF
[Interface]
PrivateKey = $U1PRIV
Address = 10.9.0.2/24

[Peer]
PublicKey = $SPUB
Endpoint = 5.188.18.103:51821
AllowedIPs = 10.9.0.0/24
PersistentKeepalive = 25
EOF

cat > /root/wg-clients/user1-bypass.conf <<EOF
[Interface]
PrivateKey = $U1PRIV
Address = 10.9.0.2/24

[Peer]
PublicKey = $SPUB
Endpoint = 5.188.18.103:51821
AllowedIPs = 0.0.0.0/1, 128.0.0.0/1
PersistentKeepalive = 25
EOF

cat > /root/wg-clients/user2-test.conf <<EOF
[Interface]
PrivateKey = $U2PRIV
Address = 10.9.0.3/24

[Peer]
PublicKey = $SPUB
Endpoint = 5.188.18.103:51821
AllowedIPs = 10.9.0.0/24
PersistentKeepalive = 25
EOF

cat > /root/wg-clients/user2-bypass.conf <<EOF
[Interface]
PrivateKey = $U2PRIV
Address = 10.9.0.3/24

[Peer]
PublicKey = $SPUB
Endpoint = 5.188.18.103:51821
AllowedIPs = 0.0.0.0/1, 128.0.0.0/1
PersistentKeepalive = 25
EOF

ping -c 2 -W 2 -I 10.9.0.1 1.1.1.1 | tail -5
echo READY
echo SPUB=$SPUB
"""

sftp = c.open_sftp()
with sftp.file("/tmp/wg_fix.sh", "w") as f:
    f.write(script)
sftp.chmod("/tmp/wg_fix.sh", 0o700)
_, o, e = c.exec_command("bash /tmp/wg_fix.sh", timeout=60)
print(o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
err = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
if err.strip():
    print("ERR", err[-1500:])

desk = pathlib.Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")
for name in ("user1-test", "user1-bypass", "user2-test", "user2-bypass"):
    data = sftp.file(f"/root/wg-clients/{name}.conf").read().decode()
    p = desk / f"wireguard-bypass-{name}.conf"
    p.write_text(data, encoding="utf-8", newline="\n")
    print("saved", p)

sftp.close()
c.close()
print("SERVER_OK")

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""OpenVPN TCP 443 fallback + verify SOCKS credentials/status."""
import pathlib
import sys

import paramiko

sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")

c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect(
    "5.188.18.103",
    username="root",
    password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi",
    timeout=25,
    allow_agent=False,
    look_for_keys=False,
)

script = r"""
set -e
# OpenVPN TCP on 1195 (443 may be nginx) - avoid clashing with web panel
cp -a /etc/openvpn/server /etc/openvpn/server-tcp 2>/dev/null || mkdir -p /etc/openvpn/server-tcp
# share certs via same paths
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
compat-mode 2.6.0
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
tls-auth /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key 0
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1400
mssfix 1360
user nobody
group nogroup
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
# no DNS push, no redirect yet
EOF

# systemd instance: openvpn-server@server-tcp expects /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf - actually unit uses config name
# Debian: /etc/openvpn/server/<name>.conf -> openvpn-server@name
systemctl enable openvpn-server@server-tcp 2>/dev/null || true
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server-tcp || {
  # fallback manual
  pkill -f 'config server-tcp.conf' || true
  /usr/local/sbin/openvpn --cd /etc/openvpn/server --config server-tcp.conf --daemon openvpn-tcp
}
sleep 1
ss -tlnp | grep 1195 || ss -tlnp | grep openvpn || true
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1
nft list chain ip nat POSTROUTING | grep -q '10.8.1.0/24' || nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname ens3 ip saddr 10.8.1.0/24 masquerade
nft list chain ip filter FORWARD | grep -q 'tun' || true

# SOCKS status
ss -tlnp | grep 1080 || true
# ensure microsocks running
if ! ss -tlnp | grep -q ':1080'; then
  # try restart known unit/cmd
  pkill microsocks || true
  nohup microsocks -p 1080 -u port -P PortKkM1080 >/var/log/microsocks.log 2>&1 &
  sleep 1
  ss -tlnp | grep 1080 || true
fi
echo TCP_SOCKS_READY
"""

sftp = c.open_sftp()
with sftp.file("/tmp/ovpn_tcp_socks.sh", "w") as f:
    f.write(script)
sftp.chmod("/tmp/ovpn_tcp_socks.sh", 0o700)
_, o, e = c.exec_command("bash /tmp/ovpn_tcp_socks.sh", timeout=45)
print(o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
print(e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")[-1000:])

# Build TCP client ovpn from existing certs
_, o, _ = c.exec_command("cat /root/openvpn-clients/user1.ovpn", timeout=15)
raw = o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
# If missing, build from easy-rsa like before
if "BEGIN CERTIFICATE" not in raw:
    raw = sftp.file("/root/openvpn-clients/user1.ovpn").read().decode()

import re
def grab(tag, text):
    m = re.search(rf"<{tag}>.*?</{tag}>", text, re.S)
    return m.group(0) if m else ""

# Prefer server client files with tls-auth
try:
    raw = sftp.file("/root/openvpn-clients/user1.ovpn").read().decode()
except Exception:
    pass

# Rebuild from PKI if needed
_, o, _ = c.exec_command(
    "TA=$(cat /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key); CA=$(cat /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt); "
    "CERT=$(awk '/BEGIN CERTIFICATE/,/END CERTIFICATE/' /etc/openvpn/easy-rsa/pki/issued/user1.crt); "
    "KEY=$(cat /etc/openvpn/easy-rsa/pki/private/user1.key); "
    "printf '%s\\n' \"$TA\" > /tmp/ta.out; printf '%s\\n' \"$CA\" > /tmp/ca.out; "
    "printf '%s\\n' \"$CERT\" > /tmp/cert.out; printf '%s\\n' \"$KEY\" > /tmp/key.out; echo ok",
    timeout=15,
)
print(o.read().decode())

ta = sftp.file("/tmp/ta.out").read().decode()
ca = sftp.file("/tmp/ca.out").read().decode()
cert = sftp.file("/tmp/cert.out").read().decode()
key = sftp.file("/tmp/key.out").read().decode()

</tls-auth>
"""
desk = pathlib.Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")
gui = pathlib.Path(r"C:\Users\cherkasov.n\OpenVPN\config")
for p in (desk / "openvpn-tcp-user1.ovpn", gui / "openvpn-tcp-user1.ovpn"):

input.contents:
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Rebuild OpenVPN on FastVPS: RSA PKI + TCP (Port-Mol style) + UDP fallback."""
from __future__ import annotations

import pathlib
import sys

import paramiko

HOST = "5.188.18.103"
PASSWORD = "W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi"
DESKTOP = pathlib.Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")

REMOTE = r"""#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
EASYRSA_DIR=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa
PKI=$EASYRSA_DIR/pki
SRV=/etc/openvpn/server
OUT=/root/openvpn-clients-rsa
WAN=$(ip -4 route show default | awk '{print $5; exit}')

echo "=== stop old openvpn instances ==="
systemctl stop openvpn-server@server openvpn-server@server-tcp 2>/dev/null || true

echo "=== ensure packages ==="
apt-get install -y -qq openvpn easy-rsa iptables >/tmp/ovpn_apt.log 2>&1 || true

echo "=== fresh RSA PKI ==="
rm -rf "$EASYRSA_DIR"
make-cadir "$EASYRSA_DIR"
cd "$EASYRSA_DIR"
# Force RSA (not EC) — matches working Port-Mol / MikroTik style
cat > vars <<'EOF'
set_var EASYRSA_ALGO rsa
set_var EASYRSA_KEY_SIZE 2048
set_var EASYRSA_CA_EXPIRE 3650
set_var EASYRSA_CERT_EXPIRE 825
set_var EASYRSA_BATCH 1
set_var EASYRSA_REQ_CN "OpenVPN-CA"
EOF
./easyrsa init-pki
./easyrsa --batch build-ca nopass
./easyrsa --batch gen-req server nopass
./easyrsa --batch sign-req server server
./easyrsa --batch gen-dh
openvpn --genkey secret "$PKI/ta.key"
for c in user1 user2; do
  ./easyrsa --batch gen-req "$c" nopass
  ./easyrsa --batch sign-req client "$c"
done

echo "=== install server certs ==="
mkdir -p "$SRV" /var/log/openvpn
cp -a "$PKI/ca.crt" "$SRV/ca.crt"
cp -a "$PKI/issued/server.crt" "$SRV/server.crt"
cp -a "$PKI/private/server.key" "$SRV/server.key"
cp -a "$PKI/dh.pem" "$SRV/dh.pem"
cp -a "$PKI/ta.key" "$SRV/ta.key"
chmod 600 "$SRV"/*.key "$SRV"/dh.pem

# Prefer system openvpn from apt if 2.6+, else local 2.7 with disable-dco
OVPN_BIN=/usr/sbin/openvpn
if "$OVPN_BIN" --version 2>&1 | head -1 | grep -q OpenVPN; then
  :
else
  OVPN_BIN=/usr/local/sbin/openvpn
fi
echo "Using binary: $($OVPN_BIN --version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Point systemd unit at binary if needed — Ubuntu unit uses /usr/sbin/openvpn
# Ensure /usr/sbin/openvpn exists (apt package)
apt-get install -y -qq openvpn >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

echo "=== write TCP primary (Port-Mol style) on 1195 ==="
cat > "$SRV/server-tcp.conf" <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
# Classic AEAD — same family as working Port-Mol configs
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-128-GCM:AES-256-CBC
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
tls-auth /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key 0
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
# Full tunnel + keep office LAN on local gateway (MikroTik)
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
# Do NOT push DNS — keep AD DNS 192.168.2.103/104
tun-mtu 1500
mssfix 1460
user nobody
group nogroup
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
EOF

echo "=== write UDP secondary on 1194 ==="
cat > "$SRV/server.conf" <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.0.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp.txt
keepalive 10 120
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-128-GCM:AES-256-CBC
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
tls-auth /etc/openvpn/server/ta.key 0
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
explicit-exit-notify 1
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
tun-mtu 1500
mssfix 1460
user nobody
group nogroup
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
EOF

echo "=== nft: ensure TCP 1195 + NAT for both pools ==="
# INPUT accept (policy already accept, but explicit)
nft list chain ip filter INPUT >/dev/null 2>&1 || nft add table ip filter
nft list chain ip filter INPUT 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'tcp dport 1195' || \
  nft add rule ip filter INPUT tcp dport 1195 accept
nft list chain ip filter INPUT 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'udp dport 1194' || \
  nft add rule ip filter INPUT udp dport 1194 accept

# FORWARD
nft list chain ip filter FORWARD 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'iifname "tun1"' || \
  nft add rule ip filter FORWARD iifname "tun1" oifname "$WAN" accept
nft list chain ip filter FORWARD 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'oifname "tun1"' || \
  nft add rule ip filter FORWARD iifname "$WAN" oifname "tun1" ct state established,related accept
nft list chain ip filter FORWARD 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'iifname "tun0"' || \
  nft add rule ip filter FORWARD iifname "tun0" oifname "$WAN" accept
nft list chain ip filter FORWARD 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'oifname "tun0"' || \
  nft add rule ip filter FORWARD iifname "$WAN" oifname "tun0" ct state established,related accept

# NAT masquerade
nft list chain ip nat POSTROUTING 2>/dev/null | grep -q '10.8.1.0/24' || \
  nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "$WAN" ip saddr 10.8.1.0/24 masquerade
nft list chain ip nat POSTROUTING 2>/dev/null | grep -q '10.8.0.0/24' || \
  nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "$WAN" ip saddr 10.8.0.0/24 masquerade

sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1 >/dev/null
grep -q 'net.ipv4.ip_forward=1' /etc/sysctl.conf || echo 'net.ipv4.ip_forward=1' >> /etc/sysctl.conf

echo "=== start services ==="
systemctl enable openvpn-server@server openvpn-server@server-tcp
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server-tcp
ss -tlnp | grep 1195 || true
ss -ulnp | grep 1194 || true
ip -br a | grep tun || true

echo "=== build client ovpn (TCP primary + UDP alt) ==="
mkdir -p "$OUT"
for c in user1 user2; do
  # Primary: TCP 1195 — same transport family as working Port-Mol
  cat > "$OUT/${c}-tcp.ovpn" <<EOC
# Bypass via FastVPS — TCP (как Port-Mol). OpenVPN GUI 2.7 или Connect.
# Локалка 192.168.2/10/20 и 10.220.220 остаётся через офисный шлюз.
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1195
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-128-GCM:AES-256-CBC
auth SHA256
tun-mtu 1500
verb 3
pull
disable-dco
windows-driver wintun
key-direction 1
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
<tls-auth>
$(cat "$PKI/ta.key")
</tls-auth>
EOC

  # UDP alternative
  cat > "$OUT/${c}-udp.ovpn" <<EOC
# Bypass via FastVPS — UDP 1194
client
dev tun
proto udp
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-128-GCM:AES-256-CBC
auth SHA256
tun-mtu 1500
verb 3
pull
disable-dco
windows-driver wintun
explicit-exit-notify 1
key-direction 1
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
<tls-auth>
$(cat "$PKI/ta.key")
</tls-auth>
EOC
done

# Connect-friendly copies without windows-driver (Connect ignores / may choke)
for c in user1 user2; do
  sed '/^windows-driver /d' "$OUT/${c}-tcp.ovpn" > "$OUT/${c}-tcp-connect.ovpn"
done

ls -la "$OUT"
echo "=== CA type check (should be rsaPublicKey) ==="
openssl x509 -in "$SRV/ca.crt" -text -noout | grep -E 'Public Key Algorithm|Subject:'
echo DONE
"""


def main() -> None:
    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    c = paramiko.SSHClient()
    c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
    c.connect(
        HOST,
        username="root",
        password=PASSWORD,
        timeout=30,
        allow_agent=False,
        look_for_keys=False,
    )
    print("Connected, running rebuild (may take ~1–2 min)...")
    _, stdout, stderr = c.exec_command(REMOTE, timeout=300)
    out = stdout.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    err = stderr.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    code = stdout.channel.recv_exit_status()
    print(out)
    if err.strip():
        print("--- stderr ---")
        print(err)
    if code != 0:
        print(f"FAILED exit={code}")
        c.close()
        sys.exit(code)

    sftp = c.open_sftp()
    remote_dir = "/root/openvpn-clients-rsa"
    DESKTOP.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    for name in sftp.listdir(remote_dir):
        if not name.endswith(".ovpn"):
            continue
        local = DESKTOP / f"bypass-{name}"
        sftp.get(f"{remote_dir}/{name}", str(local))
        print(f"Saved {local}")
    sftp.close()
    c.close()
    print("OK")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Align OpenVPN server with working MikroTik/Port-Mol style data channel."""
from __future__ import annotations

import pathlib
import sys
import time

import paramiko

HOST = "5.188.18.103"
PASSWORD = "W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi"
DESKTOP = pathlib.Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")

REMOTE = r"""#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

OVPN=$(command -v openvpn)
echo "BIN=$($OVPN --version 2>&1 | head -1)"
$OVPN --help 2>&1 | grep -iE 'compat|protocol|tls-crypt|tls-auth|auth ' | head -40 || true

# Rewrite TCP server in MikroTik-like style
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
# Match Port-Mol / RouterOS style as closely as possible
compat-mode 2.4.0
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
# Port-Mol uses NO tls-auth; keep tls-auth off for max compatibility
# (re-enable later if needed)
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
duplicate-cn
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
tun-mtu 1500
user nobody
group nogroup
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
EOF

cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server.conf <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.0.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp.txt
keepalive 10 120
compat-mode 2.4.0
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
duplicate-cn
explicit-exit-notify 1
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
tun-mtu 1500
user nobody
group nogroup
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
EOF

# Rebuild client configs WITHOUT tls-auth / key-direction
PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-clients-rsa
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
mkdir -p "$OUT"
for c in user1 user2; do
cat > "$OUT/${c}-tcp.ovpn" <<EOC
# FastVPS bypass TCP — стиль как Port-Mol (без tls-auth, compat)
# OpenVPN GUI от администратора. Локалка через офисный шлюз.
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1195
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
tun-mtu 1500
verb 3
pull
disable-dco
compat-mode 2.4.0
windows-driver tap-windows6
ip-win32 dynamic
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
EOC
done

systemctl restart openvpn-server@server openvpn-server@server-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server-tcp
# Confirm no tls-auth in running conf
grep -E 'tls-auth|compat|cipher|auth |disable' /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
ss -tlnp | grep 1195
echo DONE
"""


def main() -> None:
    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    c = paramiko.SSHClient()
    c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
    c.connect(
        HOST,
        username="root",
        password=PASSWORD,
        timeout=30,
        allow_agent=False,
        look_for_keys=False,
    )
    _, stdout, stderr = c.exec_command(REMOTE, timeout=120)
    print(stdout.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
    err = stderr.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    if err.strip():
        print("--- stderr ---")
        print(err)
    code = stdout.channel.recv_exit_status()
    if code != 0:
        sys.exit(code)

    sftp = c.open_sftp()
    for name in ("user1-tcp.ovpn", "user2-tcp.ovpn"):
        local = DESKTOP / f"bypass-{name}"
        sftp.get(f"/root/openvpn-clients-rsa/{name}", str(local))
        print(f"Saved {local}")
    sftp.close()
    c.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-status.log

verb 4
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
EOF

OUT=/root/openvpn-clients-rsa

for c in user1 user2; do
cat > "$OUT/${c}-tcp.ovpn" <<EOC

</key>

systemctl restart openvpn-server@server openvpn-server@server-tcp

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Rebuild PKI with distinct CNs (CA/server/user) — previous PKI had CN=OpenVPN-CA for all."""
from __future__ import annotations

import pathlib
import sys

import paramiko

HOST = "5.188.18.103"
PASSWORD = "W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi"
DESKTOP = pathlib.Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")

REMOTE = r"""#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

systemctl stop openvpn-server@server openvpn-server@server-tcp || true

EASYRSA_DIR=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa
rm -rf "$EASYRSA_DIR"
make-cadir "$EASYRSA_DIR"
cd "$EASYRSA_DIR"

# NO EASYRSA_REQ_CN — each cert gets its own CN
cat > vars <<'EOF'
set_var EASYRSA_ALGO rsa
set_var EASYRSA_KEY_SIZE 2048
set_var EASYRSA_CA_EXPIRE 3650
set_var EASYRSA_CERT_EXPIRE 825
set_var EASYRSA_BATCH 1
EOF

./easyrsa init-pki
./easyrsa --batch --req-cn="OpenVPN-CA" build-ca nopass
./easyrsa --batch --req-cn="server" gen-req server nopass
./easyrsa --batch sign-req server server
./easyrsa --batch gen-dh
openvpn --genkey secret pki/ta.key

for c in user1 user2; do
  ./easyrsa --batch --req-cn="$c" gen-req "$c" nopass
  ./easyrsa --batch sign-req client "$c"
done

echo '=== subjects ==='
openssl x509 -in pki/ca.crt -noout -subject
openssl x509 -in pki/issued/server.crt -noout -subject
openssl x509 -in pki/issued/user1.crt -noout -subject
openssl x509 -in pki/issued/user2.crt -noout -subject

SRV=/etc/openvpn/server
cp -a pki/ca.crt "$SRV/ca.crt"
cp -a pki/issued/server.crt "$SRV/server.crt"
cp -a pki/private/server.key "$SRV/server.key"
cp -a pki/dh.pem "$SRV/dh.pem"
cp -a pki/ta.key "$SRV/ta.key"
chmod 600 "$SRV/server.key" "$SRV/dh.pem" "$SRV/ta.key"

# Minimal TCP server like Port-Mol; add redirect after ping works
cat > "$SRV/server-tcp.conf" <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
# run as root first to rule out capability issues
# user nobody
# group nogroup
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

cat > "$SRV/server.conf" <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.0.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp.txt
keepalive 10 120
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
explicit-exit-notify 1
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

PKI=$EASYRSA_DIR/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-clients-rsa
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
mkdir -p "$OUT"
for c in user1 user2; do
cat > "$OUT/${c}-tcp.ovpn" <<EOC
# FastVPS bypass TCP (PKI fixed: distinct CN). Как Port-Mol: TLS1.2, auth none.
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1195
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth none
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
tun-mtu 1500
verb 3
pull
disable-dco
windows-driver tap-windows6
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
EOC
done

# Fix systemd to use /usr/sbin/openvpn 2.6
mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d/override.conf <<'EOF'
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/openvpn --status %t/openvpn-server/status-%i.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --config %E/openvpn/server/%i.conf
EOF
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server openvpn-server@server-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server-tcp
ss -tlnp | grep 1195
echo DONE
"""


def main() -> None:
    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    c = paramiko.SSHClient()
    c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
    c.connect(HOST, username="root", password=PASSWORD, timeout=30, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)
    print("Rebuilding PKI with distinct CNs...")
    _, o, e = c.exec_command(REMOTE, timeout=300)
    print(o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
    err = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    if err.strip():
        # easyrsa prints progress to stderr
        if "Error" in err or "error:" in err.lower():
            print(err[-2000:])
    code = o.channel.recv_exit_status()
    if code != 0:
        print(err[-3000:])
        sys.exit(code)
    sftp = c.open_sftp()
    for name in ("user1-tcp.ovpn", "user2-tcp.ovpn"):
        local = DESKTOP / f"bypass-{name}"
        sftp.get(f"/root/openvpn-clients-rsa/{name}", str(local))
        print("Saved", local)
    sftp.close()
    c.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Local loopback OpenVPN client on VPS + SoftEther install for OpenVPN compat."""
from __future__ import annotations

import pathlib
import sys

import paramiko

HOST = "5.188.18.103"
PASSWORD = "W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi"
DESKTOP = pathlib.Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")

REMOTE = r"""#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
# Local test client config pointing to 127.0.0.1
cat > /tmp/local-test.ovpn <<EOC
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote 127.0.0.1 1195
nobind
resolv-retry infinite
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth none
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
verb 3
pull
disable-dco
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/user2.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/user2.key")
</key>
EOC

# Use a separate tun name via --dev tun10 if possible - actually just tun
pkill -f 'openvpn --config /tmp/local-test' 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
/usr/sbin/openvpn --config /tmp/local-test.ovpn --daemon localtest --writepid /tmp/localtest.pid --log /tmp/localtest.log
sleep 5
echo '=== local client log ==='
cat /tmp/localtest.log
echo '=== ping from namespace/host to 10.8.1.1 ==='
# find client IP
ip -br a | grep -E 'tun|10.8'
ping -c 3 -W 2 10.8.1.1 || true
# if client got 10.8.1.x, ping it from server
CLI=$(ip -4 -o addr show | awk '/10.8.1./{print $4}' | cut -d/ -f1 | grep -v '\.1$' | head -1 || true)
echo "CLI=$CLI"
if [ -n "$CLI" ]; then ping -c 3 -W 2 "$CLI" || true; fi
echo '=== status ==='
cat /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
grep -E 'tun0|tun1|tun2' /proc/net/dev || true

# cleanup local client
kill $(cat /tmp/localtest.pid) 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
echo LOCALTEST_DONE
"""


def main() -> None:
    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    c = paramiko.SSHClient()
    c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
    c.connect(HOST, username="root", password=PASSWORD, timeout=30, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)
    _, o, e = c.exec_command(REMOTE, timeout=90)
    print(o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
    err = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    if err.strip():
        print("--- stderr ---")
        print(err[-1500:])
    c.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import sys
import time

import paramiko

sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect(
    "5.188.18.103",
    username="root",
    password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi",
    timeout=20,
    allow_agent=False,
    look_for_keys=False,
)
sftp = c.open_sftp()
sftp.put(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop\bypass-user2-tcp.ovpn", "/tmp/local-test.ovpn")
sftp.close()

remote = r"""
set +e
sed -i 's/remote 5.188.18.103 1195/remote 127.0.0.1 1195/' /tmp/local-test.ovpn
pkill -f local-test.ovpn 2>/dev/null
sleep 1
/usr/sbin/openvpn --config /tmp/local-test.ovpn --daemon localtest --writepid /tmp/localtest.pid --log /tmp/localtest.log
sleep 7
echo '=== LOG ==='
cat /tmp/localtest.log
echo '=== IPS ==='
ip -br a
echo '=== PING ==='
ping -c 3 -W 2 10.8.1.1
echo '=== DEV ==='
grep tun /proc/net/dev
echo '=== STATUS ==='
cat /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
if [ -f /tmp/localtest.pid ]; then kill $(cat /tmp/localtest.pid); fi
"""
_, o, e = c.exec_command(remote, timeout=60)
print(o.read().decode())
print(e.read().decode())
print("exit", o.channel.recv_exit_status())
c.close()

#!/bin/bash
set +e
# strip windows-only options
grep -v -E 'windows-driver|ip-win32' /tmp/local-test.ovpn > /tmp/local-test-linux.ovpn
sed -i 's/remote 5.188.18.103 1195/remote 127.0.0.1 1195/' /tmp/local-test-linux.ovpn
pkill -f local-test-linux.ovpn 2>/dev/null
# disconnect stale clients by restarting server briefly
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server-tcp
sleep 2
/usr/sbin/openvpn --config /tmp/local-test-linux.ovpn --daemon localtest --writepid /tmp/localtest.pid --log /tmp/localtest.log --dev tun15
sleep 6
echo '=== LOG ==='
cat /tmp/localtest.log
echo '=== IPS ==='
ip -br a | grep -E 'tun|10.8'
echo '=== PING gw from client IP stack ==='
# ping gateway - should work if local client got an IP
ping -c 4 -W 2 -I tun15 10.8.1.1 2>/dev/null || ping -c 4 -W 2 10.8.1.1
echo '=== DEV ==='
grep tun /proc/net/dev
echo '=== STATUS ==='
cat /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
kill $(cat /tmp/localtest.pid) 2>/dev/null
echo DONE

#!/bin/bash
set +e
# Remove redirect from server for clean test
cp -a /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf.bak
grep -v 'redirect-gateway\|push "route' /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf > /tmp/st.conf
mv /tmp/st.conf /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server-tcp
sleep 2

grep -v -E 'windows-driver|ip-win32' /tmp/local-test.ovpn > /tmp/local-test-linux.ovpn
sed -i 's/remote 5.188.18.103 1195/remote 127.0.0.1 1195/' /tmp/local-test-linux.ovpn
# also ignore any pushed redirect just in case
echo 'pull-filter ignore "redirect-gateway"' >> /tmp/local-test-linux.ovpn
echo 'route-nopull' >> /tmp/local-test-linux.ovpn
# wait route-nopull blocks ifconfig too - bad. Remove route-nopull.
sed -i '/route-nopull/d' /tmp/local-test-linux.ovpn

pkill -f local-test-linux.ovpn 2>/dev/null
/usr/sbin/openvpn --config /tmp/local-test-linux.ovpn --daemon localtest --writepid /tmp/localtest.pid --log /tmp/localtest.log --dev tun15
sleep 5
echo '=== LOG ==='
tail -30 /tmp/localtest.log
echo '=== STATUS ==='
cat /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
echo '=== PING via tun15 ==='
ping -c 5 -W 2 -I tun15 10.8.1.1
echo '=== DEV ==='
grep -E 'tun0|tun15' /proc/net/dev
kill $(cat /tmp/localtest.pid) 2>/dev/null
# restore server conf with redirect
mv /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf.bak /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server-tcp
echo DONE

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

cd /usr/local/src
if [ ! -d SoftEtherVPN ]; then
  apt-get install -y -qq build-essential cmake libncurses5-dev libreadline-dev libssl-dev zlib1g-dev git >/tmp/se_apt.log 2>&1
  git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/SoftEtherVPN/SoftEtherVPN_Stable.git SoftEtherVPN
fi
cd SoftEtherVPN
if [ ! -f /usr/local/bin/vpnserver ]; then
  ./configure
  make -j"$(nproc)"
  make install
fi

# Use prebuilt if make too heavy - try package
if [ ! -f /usr/local/vpnserver/vpnserver ] && [ ! -f /usr/bin/vpnserver ]; then
  cd /tmp
  curl -fsSL -o se.tar.gz "https://github.com/SoftEtherVPN/SoftEtherVPN_Stable/releases/download/v4.42-9798-rtm/softether-vpnserver-v4.42-9798-rtm-2023.06.30-linux-x64-64bit.tar.gz" || \
  curl -fsSL -o se.tar.gz "https://www.softether-download.com/files/softether/v4.42-9798-rtm-2023.06.30-tree/Linux/SoftEther_VPN_Server/64bit_-_Intel_x64_or_AMD64/softether-vpnserver-v4.42-9798-rtm-2023.06.30-linux-x64-64bit.tar.gz"
  tar xzf se.tar.gz
  cd vpnserver
  make
  mkdir -p /usr/local/vpnserver
  cp -a * /usr/local/vpnserver/
fi

ls -la /usr/local/vpnserver/vpnserver || ls -la /usr/local/bin/vpnserver || find /usr/local -name vpnserver 2>/dev/null | head
echo BUILD_OK

#!/bin/bash
set +e
# Switch TCP server to OpenVPN 2.7.5 local binary, auth SHA256, no auth none
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
EOF

# Force unit to use 2.7.5
mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d/override.conf <<'EOF'
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --status %t/openvpn-server/status-%i.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --config %E/openvpn/server/%i.conf
EOF
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server-tcp
sleep 2
/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --version | head -1
tr '\0' ' ' < /proc/$(pgrep -f 'server-tcp.conf')/cmdline; echo

# local client with 2.7.5 as well
grep -v -E 'windows-driver|ip-win32|auth none|tls-version-max|cipher AES' /tmp/local-test.ovpn > /tmp/lc.ovpn
sed -i 's/remote 5.188.18.103 1195/remote 127.0.0.1 1195/' /tmp/lc.ovpn
grep -q 'auth SHA256' /tmp/lc.ovpn || echo 'auth SHA256' >> /tmp/lc.ovpn
pkill -f '/tmp/lc.ovpn' 2>/dev/null
/usr/local/sbin/openvpn --config /tmp/lc.ovpn --daemon localtest --writepid /tmp/localtest.pid --log /tmp/localtest.log --dev tun15 --disable-dco
sleep 5
echo '=== LOG ==='
tail -25 /tmp/localtest.log
echo '=== RUN STATUS ==='
cat /run/openvpn-server/status-server-tcp.log 2>/dev/null || cat /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
echo '=== PING ==='
ping -c 5 -W 2 -I tun15 10.8.1.1
echo '=== DEV ==='
grep -E 'tun0|tun15' /proc/net/dev
kill $(cat /tmp/localtest.pid) 2>/dev/null
echo DONE

#!/bin/bash
set +e
# Fix rp_filter just in case
sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter=0
sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter=0
sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.tun0.rp_filter=0 2>/dev/null
sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_all=0

# Ensure TCP server healthy with 2.6, auth SHA256, redirect on
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
allow-compression no
disable-dco
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d/override.conf <<'EOF'
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/openvpn --status %t/openvpn-server/status-%i.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --config %E/openvpn/server/%i.conf
EOF
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server-tcp
sleep 2

# Install SoftEther VPN Server prebuilt
cd /tmp
if [ ! -x /usr/local/vpnserver/vpnserver ]; then
  curl -fsSL -o se.tar.gz "https://github.com/SoftEtherVPN/SoftEtherVPN_Stable/releases/download/v4.42-9798-rtm/softether-vpnserver-v4.42-9798-rtm-2023.06.30-linux-x64-64bit.tar.gz" \
    || curl -fsSL -o se.tar.gz "https://www.softether-download.com/files/softether/v4.42-9798-rtm-2023.06.30-tree/Linux/SoftEther_VPN_Server/64bit_-_Intel_x64_or_AMD64/softether-vpnserver-v4.42-9798-rtm-2023.06.30-linux-x64-64bit.tar.gz"
  tar xzf se.tar.gz
  cd vpnserver
  yes 1 | make
  mkdir -p /usr/local/vpnserver
  cp -a * /usr/local/vpnserver/
  chmod 600 /usr/local/vpnserver/*
  chmod 700 /usr/local/vpnserver/vpnserver /usr/local/vpnserver/vpncmd
fi
echo SE_BIN_OK
ls -la /usr/local/vpnserver/vpnserver

#!/bin/bash
set +e
sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter=0
sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter=0

# Classic CBC like older OpenVPN / closer to dumb clients
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
# Force legacy data channel — avoid AEAD+dyn-tls-crypt path
compat-mode 2.3.0
data-ciphers AES-256-CBC
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-CBC
cipher AES-256-CBC
auth SHA1
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
disable-dco
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d/override.conf <<'EOF'
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/openvpn --status %t/openvpn-server/status-%i.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --config %E/openvpn/server/%i.conf
EOF
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server-tcp

PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-clients-rsa
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
for c in user1 user2; do
cat > "$OUT/${c}-tcp.ovpn" <<EOC
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1195
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
data-ciphers AES-256-CBC
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-CBC
cipher AES-256-CBC
auth SHA1
compat-mode 2.3.0
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
tun-mtu 1500
verb 3
pull
disable-dco
windows-driver tap-windows6
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
echo DONE
ls -la $OUT/*tcp.ovpn

$ovpn='E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop\bypass-user1-tcp.ovpn'
$log='E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop\ovpn-test-connect.log'

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
cd /usr/local/src
if [ ! -x /usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn ]; then
  apt-get install -y -qq build-essential libssl-dev liblzo2-dev libpam0g-dev liblz4-dev pkg-config >/tmp/ov25apt.log
  curl -fsSL -o openvpn-2.5.10.tar.gz https://swupdate.openvpn.org/community/releases/openvpn-2.5.10.tar.gz
  tar xzf openvpn-2.5.10.tar.gz
  cd openvpn-2.5.10
  ./configure --prefix=/usr/local/openvpn-2.5 --disable-dco 2>/tmp/ov25conf.log
  make -j"$(nproc)"
  make install
fi
/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --version | head -2

# Server config for 2.5
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d/override.conf <<'EOF'
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --status %t/openvpn-server/status-%i.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --config %E/openvpn/server/%i.conf
EOF
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server-tcp
tr '\0' ' ' < /proc/$(pgrep -f 'server-tcp.conf' | head -1)/cmdline; echo

PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-clients-rsa
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
for c in user1 user2; do
cat > "$OUT/${c}-tcp.ovpn" <<EOC
# FastVPS bypass — сервер OpenVPN 2.5 (без dyn-tls-crypt), как рабочие чужие .ovpn
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1195
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
tun-mtu 1500
verb 3
pull
disable-dco
windows-driver tap-windows6
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
echo DONE

#!/bin/bash
set +e
# OpenVPN 2.5-compatible config (no disable-dco, no data-ciphers)
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

# Test start manually first
pkill -f 'openvpn.*server-tcp' 2>/dev/null
sleep 1
/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --config /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf --daemon ovpn25tcp --writepid /run/openvpn25-tcp.pid
sleep 2
ps aux | grep '[o]vpn25\|openvpn-2.5'
ss -tlnp | grep 1195
tail -20 /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log

# Update systemd to not timeout - use Type=forking with our daemon? Or just keep manual for now
# Better: fix unit TimeoutStartSec and use openvpn 2.5 without --daemon (systemd expects foreground)
mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service.d/override.conf <<'EOF'
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --status %t/openvpn-server/status-%i.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --config %E/openvpn/server/%i.conf
TimeoutStartSec=30
EOF
# kill manual, use systemd
kill $(cat /run/openvpn25-tcp.pid) 2>/dev/null
sleep 1
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl restart openvpn-server@server-tcp
sleep 3
systemctl is-active openvpn-server@server-tcp
ss -tlnp | grep 1195
tr '\0' ' ' < /proc/$(pgrep -f 'server-tcp.conf' | head -1)/cmdline; echo
tail -15 /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
echo DONE

#!/bin/bash
set +e
# Stop broken unit
systemctl stop openvpn-server@server-tcp 2>/dev/null
pkill -f 'openvpn.*server-tcp' 2>/dev/null
pkill -f openvpn-2.5 2>/dev/null
sleep 1

cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

# Dedicated unit that works with OpenVPN 2.5
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn25-tcp.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=OpenVPN 2.5 TCP bypass server
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=forking
PIDFile=/run/openvpn25-tcp.pid
ExecStart=/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --daemon openvpn25-tcp --writepid /run/openvpn25-tcp.pid --config /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=3

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl disable openvpn-server@server-tcp 2>/dev/null
systemctl enable --now openvpn25-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn25-tcp
ss -tlnp | grep 1195
/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --version | head -1
tail -8 /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log

# client configs GCM for 2.5 server
PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-clients-rsa
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
for c in user1 user2; do
cat > "$OUT/${c}-tcp.ovpn" <<EOC
# FastVPS bypass — OpenVPN 2.5 server (без dyn-tls-crypt). OpenVPN GUI от админа.
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1195
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth SHA256
tun-mtu 1500
verb 3
pull
disable-dco
windows-driver tap-windows6
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
echo DONE

#!/bin/bash
set +e
# Force TLS1.2 like Port-Mol
sed -i '/tls-version-min/d;/tls-version-max/d' /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
echo 'tls-version-min 1.2' >> /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
echo 'tls-version-max 1.2' >> /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
# auth none like Port-Mol
sed -i 's/^auth .*/auth none/' /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
grep -q '^auth ' /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf || echo 'auth none' >> /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
systemctl restart openvpn25-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn25-tcp
cat /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
# Enable full tunnel again on server
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1195
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.8.1.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
duplicate-cn
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "block-ipv6"
push "ifconfig-ipv6 fd00::1/64 fd00::"
EOF
# block-ipv6 push might need different syntax on 2.5 - if start fails remove
systemctl restart openvpn25-tcp || {
  # remove ipv6 lines if fail
  sed -i '/block-ipv6\|ifconfig-ipv6/d' /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
  systemctl restart openvpn25-tcp
}
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn25-tcp

for c in user1 user2; do
cat > /root/openvpn-clients-rsa/${c}-tcp.ovpn <<EOC
# FastVPS bypass — структура как Port-Mol (рабочий .ovpn)
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1195
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
ping 15
ping-restart 45
persist-tun
persist-key
mute-replay-warnings
verb 3
cipher AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
pull
remote-cert-tls server
reneg-sec 3600
block-ipv6
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
echo DONE
ls -la /root/openvpn-clients-rsa/*tcp.ovpn

input.contents:
#!/bin/bash
set +e
# OpenVPN 2.5 UDP — TCP data plane may be mangled by office MikroTik
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server.conf <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.220.222.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp.txt
keepalive 10 120
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
explicit-exit-notify 1
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

WAN=$(ip -4 route show default | awk '{print $5; exit}')
nft list chain ip nat POSTROUTING | grep -q '10.220.222.0/24' || \
  nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "$WAN" ip saddr 10.220.222.0/24 masquerade

cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn25-udp.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=OpenVPN 2.5 UDP bypass
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target
[Service]
Type=forking
PIDFile=/run/openvpn25-udp.pid
ExecStart=/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --daemon openvpn25-udp --writepid /run/openvpn25-udp.pid --config /etc/openvpn/server/server.conf
Restart=on-failure
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
systemctl disable --now openvpn-server@server 2>/dev/null
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now openvpn25-udp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn25-udp
ss -ulnp | grep 1194

PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
for c in user1 user2; do
cat > /root/openvpn-clients-rsa/${c}-udp.ovpn <<EOC
# FastVPS bypass UDP — если TCP режет MikroTik
client
dev tun
proto udp
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
ping 15
ping-restart 45
persist-tun
persist-key
mute-replay-warnings
verb 3
cipher AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
pull
remote-cert-tls server
explicit-exit-notify 1
block-ipv6
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
echo DONE

content.text:
Разобрал рабочие Port-Mol `.ovpn` и пересобрал сервер под них.

**Что выяснил:** у тебя Port-Mol на этом же ПК **реально гоняет трафик** (`ping 10.220.220.254` ок, в логе куча `TUN READ`). Значит TAP/клиент живые. Чужие конфиги — MikroTik: TCP, RSA, `auth none`, TLS1.2, **без** `dyn-tls-crypt`.

**Что сделал на VPS:**
- поставил **OpenVPN 2.5.10** (убрал `dyn-tls-crypt` из 2.6/2.7);
- новая RSA PKI с нормальными CN;
- TCP `1195` / UDP `1194`, full-tunnel + исключения офисной сети;
- конфиги на рабочем столе.

**Файлы:**
- `bypass-user1-tcp.ovpn` / `bypass-user2-tcp.ovpn` — основные  
- `bypass-user1-udp.ovpn` / `bypass-user2-udp.ovpn` — запас  
- `OpenVPN-bypass-ЧИТАЙ.txt` — инструкция  
- `SOCKS-обход-РАБОЧИЙ.txt` — **уже работает** (`5.188.18.103:1080`)

**Честно:** сессия нашего OpenVPN поднимается (TLS + PUSH как у Port-Mol), но `ping` до VPN-шлюза с этого ПК всё ещё не проходит — data plane через офисный MikroTik к Linux-OpenVPN пока ломается, хотя к самому MikroTik-VPN — нет. SOCKS через тот же VPS при этом ок.

Сделай так: **OpenVPN GUI от администратора** → import `bypass-user1-tcp.ovpn` → Connect → `ping 10.220.221.1` и ipify. Если снова тишина — для YouTube/браузера бери SOCKS, а `.ovpn` добьём (SoftEther или проверка с домашней сети в обход MikroTik).
