content.text: Ставлю SoftEther как обычно для Connect (клон OpenVPN), порт 1080 и nginx не трогаю.

input.contents:
#!/bin/bash
# SoftEther OpenVPN for OpenVPN Connect — do NOT touch microsocks :1080 or nginx :443
set -euo pipefail
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

SE_DIR=/usr/local/vpnserver
SE_PASS='BypassSe!2026'
HUB=BYPASS
USER1=user1
USER2=user2
USERPASS='BypassVpn!1080'
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103

echo "=== preserve SOCKS check ==="
ss -tlnp | grep -q ':1080' || { echo "FATAL: microsocks not listening"; exit 1; }

echo "=== stop our OpenVPN 2.5 (free 1194/1195); leave SOCKS alone ==="
systemctl stop openvpn25-tcp openvpn25-udp 2>/dev/null || true
systemctl disable openvpn25-tcp openvpn25-udp 2>/dev/null || true
systemctl stop openvpn-server@server openvpn-server@server-tcp 2>/dev/null || true
systemctl disable openvpn-server@server openvpn-server@server-tcp 2>/dev/null || true
pkill -f 'openvpn.*server' 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1

echo "=== install SoftEther if needed ==="
if [ ! -x "$SE_DIR/vpnserver" ]; then
  apt-get install -y -qq build-essential curl unzip >/tmp/se_apt.log
  cd /tmp
  rm -rf vpnserver se.tar.gz
  curl -fsSL -o se.tar.gz \
    "https://github.com/SoftEtherVPN/SoftEtherVPN_Stable/releases/download/v4.42-9798-rtm/softether-vpnserver-v4.42-9798-rtm-2023.06.30-linux-x64-64bit.tar.gz" \
    || curl -fsSL -o se.tar.gz \
    "https://www.softether-download.com/files/softether/v4.42-9798-rtm-2023.06.30-tree/Linux/SoftEther_VPN_Server/64bit_-_Intel_x64_or_AMD64/softether-vpnserver-v4.42-9798-rtm-2023.06.30-linux-x64-64bit.tar.gz"
  tar xzf se.tar.gz
  cd vpnserver
  printf '1\n1\n1\n' | make
  mkdir -p "$SE_DIR"
  cp -a * "$SE_DIR/"
  chmod 600 "$SE_DIR"/*
  chmod 700 "$SE_DIR/vpnserver" "$SE_DIR/vpncmd"
fi

cat > /etc/systemd/system/softether-vpnserver.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=SoftEther VPN Server
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target
[Service]
Type=forking
ExecStart=/usr/local/vpnserver/vpnserver start
ExecStop=/usr/local/vpnserver/vpnserver stop
Restart=on-failure
WorkingDirectory=/usr/local/vpnserver
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now softether-vpnserver
sleep 3

VPNCMD="$SE_DIR/vpncmd"
# First-time admin password (empty -> set)
$VPNCMD localhost /SERVER /CMD ServerPasswordSet "$SE_PASS" <<EOF || true
$SE_PASS
$SE_PASS
EOF

# Configure via non-interactive cmds
cfg() { $VPNCMD localhost /SERVER /PASSWORD:"$SE_PASS" /CMD "$@"; }

# Free 443 for nginx — SoftEther must NOT steal HTTPS
cfg ListenerList || true
cfg ListenerDelete 443 || true
cfg ListenerDelete 992 || true
# keep 5555 for admin (can restrict later)

# Hub + users
cfg HubCreate "$HUB" /PASSWORD:"" || true
cfg Hub "$HUB"
cfg SecureNatEnable
cfg DhcpSet /START:10.220.223.10 /END:10.220.223.50 /MASK:255.255.255.0 /EXPIRE:7200 /GW:10.220.223.1 /DNS:8.8.8.8 /DNS2:1.1.1.1 /DOMAIN:bypass.local /LOG:yes || true
cfg UserCreate "$USER1" /GROUP:none /REALNAME:user1 /NOTE:bypass || true
cfg UserPasswordSet "$USER1" /PASSWORD:"$USERPASS"
cfg UserCreate "$USER2" /GROUP:none /REALNAME:user2 /NOTE:bypass || true
cfg UserPasswordSet "$USER2" /PASSWORD:"$USERPASS"

# OpenVPN clone on 1194 (UDP) and 1195 (TCP) — classic ports
cfg OpenVpnEnable yes /PORTS:1194
# SoftEther OpenVPN also supports TCP via proto options — set both if available
cfg ProtoOptionsSet /NAME:OpenVPN_UdpPortList /VALUE:1194 || true
cfg ProtoOptionsSet /NAME:OpenVPN_TcpPortList /VALUE:1195 || true
cfg OpenVpnEnable yes /PORTS:1194,1195 || cfg OpenVpnEnable yes /PORTS:1194

# NAT / IP forward
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1 >/dev/null
WAN=$(ip -4 route show default | awk '{print $5; exit}')
nft list chain ip nat POSTROUTING 2>/dev/null | grep -q '10.220.223.0/24' || \
  nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "$WAN" ip saddr 10.220.223.0/24 masquerade
nft list chain ip filter INPUT 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'udp dport 1194' || \
  nft add rule ip filter INPUT udp dport 1194 accept
nft list chain ip filter INPUT 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'tcp dport 1195' || \
  nft add rule ip filter INPUT tcp dport 1195 accept

# Export OpenVPN configs
rm -f /tmp/softether_ovpn.zip /tmp/se_ovpn/*
mkdir -p /tmp/se_ovpn /root/openvpn-connect
cfg OpenVpnMakeConfig /tmp/softether_ovpn.zip
unzip -o /tmp/softether_ovpn.zip -d /tmp/se_ovpn
ls -la /tmp/se_ovpn

# Build Connect-friendly profiles (Port-Mol layout) from SoftEther CA + user/pass
# SoftEther zip usually contains *_openvpn_remote_access_l3.ovpn or similar
SRC=$(find /tmp/se_ovpn -name '*.ovpn' | head -1)
echo "SRC=$SRC"
# Extract ca/cert from sample if present; SoftEther uses auth-user-pass
CA_BLOCK=$(awk '/<ca>/,/<\/ca>/' "$SRC")

for u in user1 user2; do
  # TCP primary — like Port-Mol for Connect
  cat > /root/openvpn-connect/${u}-connect-tcp.ovpn <<EOC
# FastVPS bypass for OpenVPN Connect (образец Port-Mol)
# Локалка 192.168.2/10/20 и 10.220.220 — через офисный шлюз

client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1195
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
ping 15
ping-restart 45
persist-tun
persist-key
mute-replay-warnings
verb 3
cipher AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-256-CBC
auth none
pull
auth-user-pass
reneg-sec 3600
remote-cert-tls server
connect-retry 2
connect-retry-max 10

# офис не в туннель
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
ignore-unknown-option block-outside-dns

<auth-user-pass>
${u}
${USERPASS}
</auth-user-pass>

${CA_BLOCK}
EOC

  # UDP alternate
  cat > /root/openvpn-connect/${u}-connect-udp.ovpn <<EOC
# FastVPS bypass for OpenVPN Connect — UDP
client
dev tun
proto udp
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
ping 15
ping-restart 45
persist-tun
persist-key
mute-replay-warnings
verb 3
cipher AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-256-CBC
auth none
pull
auth-user-pass
reneg-sec 3600
remote-cert-tls server
explicit-exit-notify 1

route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
ignore-unknown-option block-outside-dns

<auth-user-pass>
${u}
${USERPASS}
</auth-user-pass>

${CA_BLOCK}
EOC
done

# Also copy SoftEther's own sample as-is for Connect
cp -a "$SRC" /root/openvpn-connect/softether-sample.ovpn

echo "=== verify SOCKS still up ==="
ss -tlnp | grep 1080
ps aux | grep '[m]icrosocks'
echo "=== softether / openvpn ports ==="
ss -tlnp | grep -E '1195|5555|443' | head -20
ss -ulnp | grep 1194 || true
echo "=== nginx 443 still nginx? ==="
ss -tlnp | grep ':443'
ls -la /root/openvpn-connect/
echo DONE

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
SE_DIR=/usr/local/vpnserver
SE_PASS='BypassSe!2026'
HUB=BYPASS
USERPASS='BypassVpn!1080'
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
VPNCMD="$SE_DIR/vpncmd"

# After ListenerDelete 443, admin is on 5555
cfg() { $VPNCMD "localhost:5555" /SERVER /PASSWORD:"$SE_PASS" /CMD "$@"; }
cfg_hub() { $VPNCMD "localhost:5555" /SERVER /PASSWORD:"$SE_PASS" /HUB:"$HUB" /CMD "$@"; }

echo "=== SOCKS guard ==="
systemctl is-active --quiet microsocks || systemctl start microsocks

echo "=== listeners ==="
cfg ListenerList
# keep 1194 for OpenVPN TCP (Port-Mol style), 5555 admin, remove 992 if want
cfg ListenerDelete 992 || true

echo "=== hub/users ==="
cfg HubCreate "$HUB" /PASSWORD:"" || true
cfg_hub SecureNatEnable || true
cfg_hub DhcpSet /START:10.220.223.10 /END:10.220.223.50 /MASK:255.255.255.0 /EXPIRE:7200 /GW:10.220.223.1 /DNS:8.8.8.8 /DNS2:1.1.1.1 /DOMAIN:bypass.local /LOG:yes || true
cfg_hub UserCreate user1 /GROUP:none /REALNAME:user1 /NOTE:bypass || true
cfg_hub UserPasswordSet user1 /PASSWORD:"$USERPASS"
cfg_hub UserCreate user2 /GROUP:none /REALNAME:user2 /NOTE:bypass || true
cfg_hub UserPasswordSet user2 /PASSWORD:"$USERPASS"

echo "=== OpenVPN enable ==="
cfg OpenVpnEnable yes /PORTS:1194
cfg OpenVpnStatus

echo "=== NAT ==="
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1 >/dev/null
WAN=$(ip -4 route show default | awk '{print $5; exit}')
nft list chain ip nat POSTROUTING 2>/dev/null | grep -q '10.220.223.0/24' || \
  nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "$WAN" ip saddr 10.220.223.0/24 masquerade
nft list chain ip filter FORWARD 2>/dev/null | grep -q '10.220.223' || \
  nft add rule ip filter FORWARD ip saddr 10.220.223.0/24 accept || true

echo "=== export ovpn ==="
rm -rf /tmp/se_ovpn /tmp/softether_ovpn.zip
mkdir -p /tmp/se_ovpn /root/openvpn-connect
cfg OpenVpnMakeConfig /tmp/softether_ovpn.zip
unzip -o /tmp/softether_ovpn.zip -d /tmp/se_ovpn
ls -la /tmp/se_ovpn
SRC=$(find /tmp/se_ovpn -name '*_openvpn_*.ovpn' | head -1)
# prefer remote_access_l3
SRC=$(find /tmp/se_ovpn -name '*remote_access_l3*.ovpn' | head -1)
[ -n "$SRC" ] || SRC=$(find /tmp/se_ovpn -name '*.ovpn' | head -1)
echo "SRC=$SRC"
head -40 "$SRC"

CA_BLOCK=$(awk '/<ca>/,/<\/ca>/' "$SRC")
# SoftEther sample may include cert/key - for user/pass we only need CA

for u in user1 user2; do
cat > /root/openvpn-connect/${u}-connect.ovpn <<EOC
# FastVPS — OpenVPN Connect (как Port-Mol)
# Сервер SoftEther OpenVPN clone, TCP 1194
# Локалка остаётся через офис

client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
ping 15
ping-restart 45
persist-tun
persist-key
mute-replay-warnings
verb 3
cipher AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-256-CBC
auth none
pull
auth-user-pass
reneg-sec 3600
remote-cert-tls server
connect-retry 2
connect-retry-max 10

route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
ignore-unknown-option block-outside-dns

<auth-user-pass>
${u}
${USERPASS}
</auth-user-pass>

${CA_BLOCK}
EOC
done

# Also keep SoftEther original sample (edit remote host)
cp -a "$SRC" /root/openvpn-connect/softether-sample.ovpn
sed -i "s/remote .* /remote ${PUBLIC_IP} /" /root/openvpn-connect/softether-sample.ovpn || true

echo "=== final ports ==="
ss -tlnp | grep -E '1080|1194|443|5555' || true
ss -ulnp | grep 1194 || true
echo "nginx443:"
ss -tlnp | grep ':443 ' || true
systemctl is-active microsocks softether-vpnserver
ls -la /root/openvpn-connect/
echo DONE

#!/bin/bash
set +e
SE=/usr/local/vpnserver
PASS='BypassSe!2026'
HUB=BYPASS
UPASS='BypassVpn!1080'
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103

# Kill stuck vpncmd

sleep 2

cfg() {
  timeout 30 $SE/vpncmd localhost:5555 /SERVER /PASSWORD:"$PASS" /CMD "$@"
  echo "CMD $* -> $?"
}

cfg HubList
cfg HubCreate "$HUB" /PASSWORD:none
# empty hub password for openvpn simplicity - HubPasswordSet if needed

# Select hub commands need /HUB:
hub() {
  timeout 45 $SE/vpncmd localhost:5555 /SERVER /PASSWORD:"$PASS" /HUB:"$HUB" /CMD "$@"

}

hub UserPasswordSet user1 /PASSWORD:"$UPASS"
hub UserCreate user2 /GROUP:none /REALNAME:u2 /NOTE:x

input.contents:
#!/bin/bash
# Minimal SoftEther setup via /IN batch — no shell-special passwords
set +e
SE=/usr/local/vpnserver
pkill -9 vpncmd 2>/dev/null
sleep 1

# Try both old and new admin pass
for P in 'BypassSe2026' 'BypassSe!2026' ''; do
  if [ -z "$P" ]; then
    timeout 12 $SE/vpncmd localhost:5555 /SERVER /CMD ListenerList </dev/null && break
  else
    timeout 12 $SE/vpncmd localhost:5555 /SERVER /PASSWORD:"$P" /CMD ListenerList </dev/null && { ADMIN="$P"; echo "ADMIN_OK=$ADMIN"; break; }
  fi
done
echo "using ADMIN=${ADMIN:-EMPTY}"

# write batch
cat > /tmp/se_batch.txt <<'BAT'
HubCreate BYPASS /PASSWORD:hubpass
Hub BYPASS
SecureNatEnable
UserCreate user1 /GROUP:none /REALNAME:user1 /NOTE:bypass
UserPasswordSet user1 /PASSWORD:BypassVpn1080
UserCreate user2 /GROUP:none /REALNAME:user2 /NOTE:bypass
UserPasswordSet user2 /PASSWORD:BypassVpn1080
UserList
OpenVpnEnable yes /PORTS:1194
OpenVpnMakeConfig /tmp/softether_ovpn.zip
BAT

# Hub commands after Hub BYPASS are in hub context when using interactive;
# with /IN, need separate approach. Use one command at a time with timeout.

ADMIN=${ADMIN:-BypassSe2026}
run() { echo "RUN: $*"; timeout 20 $SE/vpncmd localhost:5555 /SERVER /PASSWORD:"$ADMIN" /CMD "$@" </dev/null; echo rc=$?; }

run HubList
run "HubCreate BYPASS /PASSWORD:hubpass"
run "Hub BYPASS"
# hub-scoped via /HUB:
run_h() { echo "HUB: $*"; timeout 20 $SE/vpncmd localhost:5555 /SERVER /PASSWORD:"$ADMIN" /HUB:BYPASS /CMD "$@" </dev/null; echo rc=$?; }
run_h SecureNatEnable
run_h "UserCreate user1 /GROUP:none /REALNAME:user1 /NOTE:x"
run_h "UserPasswordSet user1 /PASSWORD:BypassVpn1080"
run_h "UserCreate user2 /GROUP:none /REALNAME:user2 /NOTE:x"
run_h "UserPasswordSet user2 /PASSWORD:BypassVpn1080"
run_h UserList
run "OpenVpnEnable yes /PORTS:1194"
run OpenVpnStatus
run "OpenVpnMakeConfig /tmp/softether_ovpn.zip"
ls -la /tmp/softether_ovpn.zip
echo DONE_BATCH

input.contents:
#!/bin/bash
set +e
# Stop SoftEther (hung vpncmd); keep microsocks; start OpenVPN 2.5 TCP for Connect
pkill -9 vpncmd 2>/dev/null
systemctl stop softether-vpnserver 2>/dev/null
systemctl disable softether-vpnserver 2>/dev/null

systemctl is-active --quiet microsocks || systemctl start microsocks

# OpenVPN 2.5 TCP on 1194 (freed) — Port-Mol uses 1194/tcp
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.220.221.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 120
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

# Ensure unit exists
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn25-tcp.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=OpenVPN 2.5 TCP for Connect
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target
[Service]
Type=forking
PIDFile=/run/openvpn25-tcp.pid
ExecStart=/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --daemon openvpn25-tcp --writepid /run/openvpn25-tcp.pid --config /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1 >/dev/null
WAN=$(ip -4 route show default | awk '{print $5; exit}')
nft list chain ip nat POSTROUTING 2>/dev/null | grep -q '10.220.221.0/24' || \
  nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "$WAN" ip saddr 10.220.221.0/24 masquerade
nft list chain ip filter INPUT 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'tcp dport 1194' || \
  nft add rule ip filter INPUT tcp dport 1194 accept

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now openvpn25-tcp
sleep 2

PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-connect
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
mkdir -p "$OUT"

for c in user1 user2; do
# Exact Port-Mol Connect layout (cert auth instead of user-pass)
cat > "$OUT/${c}-connect.ovpn" <<EOC
# FastVPS bypass — OpenVPN Connect
# Образец: Port-Mol (TCP 1194, AES-256-GCM, auth none, pull)

client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
ping 15
ping-restart 45
persist-tun
persist-key
mute-replay-warnings
verb 3
cipher AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-256-CBC
auth none
pull
reneg-sec 3600
remote-cert-tls server
connect-retry 2
connect-retry-max 10

# офис / домен — не через VPS
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
ignore-unknown-option block-outside-dns

<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
EOC
done

echo "=== status ==="
systemctl is-active openvpn25-tcp microsocks
ss -tlnp | grep -E '1080|1194'
/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --version | head -1
ls -la "$OUT"
# quick local socks check
curl -s --max-time 5 -x socks5h://port:PortKkM1080@127.0.0.1:1080 http://api.ipify.org; echo
echo DONE

<mcp_meta_tools>
You have access to MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools through `GetMcpTools` and `CallMcpTool`.

## MCP Tool Discovery and Invocation

Use `GetMcpTools` to discover tool schemas, then `CallMcpTool` to invoke them. Aim to minimize round-trips: ideally one `GetMcpTools` call followed by one `CallMcpTool` call.

If the user mentions, references, or links to a product or service that corresponds to an available MCP server, and the request likely depends on information from that service, proactively inspect that MCP server before answering. Do not wait for the user to explicitly ask you to use MCP. If you are unsure which server matches, use `GetMcpTools` with a pattern based on the service name.

`GetMcpTools` supports four modes:

1. `{"server":"<id>"}`: returns full input schemas and full descriptions for every tool on that server. Preferred when you know which server to use.
2. `{"server":"<id>","toolName":"<name>"}`: returns the full schema and full description for one tool.
3. `{"pattern":"<regex>"}`: searches tool and server names across all servers using RE2 syntax (no backreferences, lookahead, or lookbehind). Use when you're unsure which server has the tool you need.

input.contents:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-connect
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
mkdir -p "$OUT"

# Server: no DNS push (Connect was forcing 8.8.8.8 and breaking AD/path)
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.220.221.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 60
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
# full tunnel; office stays local
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
# DO NOT push DNS
EOF
systemctl restart openvpn25-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn25-tcp microsocks

for c in user1 user2; do
cat > "$OUT/${c}-connect.ovpn" <<EOC
# FastVPS — OpenVPN Connect (образец Port-Mol + фикс маршрута к серверу)
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
ping 15
ping-restart 45
persist-tun
persist-key
persist-remote-ip
mute-replay-warnings
verb 3
cipher AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-256-CBC
auth none
pull
reneg-sec 3600
remote-cert-tls server
connect-retry 2
connect-retry-max 10

# критично: TCP к VPS не уводить в туннель (иначе NETWORK_SEND_ERROR)
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway

# офис — локальный шлюз (если сервер уже пушит — дубль безвреден)
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway

# не принимать чужой DNS (AD)
pull-filter ignore "dhcp-option DNS"
pull-filter ignore "dhcp-option DNS6"
pull-filter ignore "block-outside-dns"
ignore-unknown-option block-outside-dns

<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${c}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${c}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
ls -la "$OUT"
wc -c "$OUT"/*.ovpn
echo DONE

input.contents:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-connect
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
mkdir -p "$OUT"

# Back to known-good server (handshake worked). No push tun-mtu/mssfix.
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.220.221.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 60
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

systemctl restart openvpn25-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn25-tcp

for cname in user1 user2; do
  cat > "$OUT/${cname}-connect.ovpn" <<EOC
# FastVPS — OpenVPN Connect (как Port-Mol)
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
auth none
cipher AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
verb 3
# TCP к VPS не уводить в туннель
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
ls -la "$OUT"
echo DONE

# Diagnose: office nets via dead TAP break path to gateway 192.168.2.1
Write-Host '=== ALL 192.168 / 10.220 routes ==='
Get-NetRoute -AddressFamily IPv4 -EA SilentlyContinue | Where-Object {
  $_.DestinationPrefix -match '^(192\.168\.|10\.220\.|5\.188\.)'
} | Sort-Object DestinationPrefix | Format-Table DestinationPrefix,NextHop,InterfaceAlias,RouteMetric,ifIndex -AutoSize

Write-Host '=== TAP admin state ==='
Get-NetAdapter | Where-Object { $_.InterfaceDescription -match 'TAP|OpenVPN|Radmin' } | Format-Table Name,Status,AdminStatus,MediaConnectionState,ifIndex -AutoSize

# Remove office routes that point into TAP adapters (wrong)
$tapIdx = (Get-NetAdapter | Where-Object { $_.InterfaceDescription -match 'TAP-Windows|Data Channel Offload' }).ifIndex
Get-NetRoute -AddressFamily IPv4 -EA SilentlyContinue | Where-Object {
  $_.InterfaceIndex -in $tapIdx -and (
    $_.DestinationPrefix -like '192.168.*' -or
    $_.DestinationPrefix -like '10.220.220.*' -or
    $_.DestinationPrefix -in @('0.0.0.0/1','128.0.0.0/1')
  )
} | ForEach-Object {
  Write-Host "REMOVE $($_.DestinationPrefix) via $($_.NextHop) if=$($_.InterfaceAlias)"
  Remove-NetRoute -DestinationPrefix $_.DestinationPrefix -InterfaceIndex $_.InterfaceIndex -NextHop $_.NextHop -Confirm:$false -EA SilentlyContinue
}

# Ensure Ethernet has correct local routes
$eth = Get-NetAdapter | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq 'Up' -and $_.Name -match 'Ethernet' } | Select-Object -First 1
Write-Host "Primary eth: $($eth.Name) idx=$($eth.ifIndex)"
# Show gateway
Get-NetIPConfiguration -InterfaceIndex $eth.ifIndex | Format-List InterfaceAlias,IPv4Address,IPv4DefaultGateway

Write-Host '=== after cleanup 192.168 routes ==='
Get-NetRoute -AddressFamily IPv4 -EA SilentlyContinue | Where-Object {
  $_.DestinationPrefix -match '^(192\.168\.|10\.220\.|5\.188\.|0\.0\.0\.0/1|128\.0\.0\.0/1)'
} | Format-Table DestinationPrefix,NextHop,InterfaceAlias,RouteMetric -AutoSize

# Can we reach VPS and gateway?
tnc 192.168.2.1 -WarningAction SilentlyContinue | Select ComputerName,PingSucceeded
tnc 5.188.18.103 -Port 1194 -WarningAction SilentlyContinue | Select TcpTestSucceeded

input.contents:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-connect
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
mkdir -p "$OUT"

# Keep server as-is (working TLS). Optionally allow duplicate-cn for tests.
grep -q duplicate-cn /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf || echo 'duplicate-cn' >> /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
systemctl restart openvpn25-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn25-tcp

for cname in user1 user2; do
  cat > "$OUT/${cname}-connect.ovpn" <<EOC
# FastVPS — OpenVPN Connect / TAP only (DCO ломает трафик)
client
dev tun
dev-node OpenVPN TAP-Windows6
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
auth none
cipher AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
verb 3
# ОБЯЗАТЕЛЬНО до любых прочих опций
ignore-unknown-option disable-dco
disable-dco
route-method exe
route-delay 2
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
ls -la "$OUT"
echo DONE

# Kill leftovers, disable DCO adapters, clean IPs/routes
Get-Process openvpn -EA SilentlyContinue | Stop-Process -Force -EA SilentlyContinue
Start-Sleep 1

Get-NetAdapter | Where-Object { $_.InterfaceDescription -match 'Data Channel Offload' } | ForEach-Object {
  Write-Host "Disable DCO: $($_.Name)"
  Disable-NetAdapter -Name $_.Name -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
}

# Clear stale IPs on TAP
Get-NetAdapter | Where-Object { $_.InterfaceDescription -match 'TAP-Windows' } | ForEach-Object {
  Enable-NetAdapter -Name $_.Name -Confirm:$false -EA SilentlyContinue
  Get-NetIPAddress -InterfaceIndex $_.ifIndex -AddressFamily IPv4 -EA SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.PrefixOrigin -ne 'WellKnown' } | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "Remove IP $($_.IPAddress) from $($_.InterfaceAlias)"
    Remove-NetIPAddress -InterfaceIndex $_.InterfaceIndex -IPAddress $_.IPAddress -Confirm:$false -EA SilentlyContinue
  }
  Remove-NetRoute -InterfaceIndex $_.ifIndex -Confirm:$false -EA SilentlyContinue
}

Get-NetRoute -DestinationPrefix '0.0.0.0/1' -EA SilentlyContinue | Remove-NetRoute -Confirm:$false -EA SilentlyContinue
Get-NetRoute -DestinationPrefix '128.0.0.0/1' -EA SilentlyContinue | Remove-NetRoute -Confirm:$false -EA SilentlyContinue

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
# Switch data channel to classic CBC+SHA256 (MikroTik-like), test from remote via tcpdump

cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.220.221.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 60
# classic crypto like many MikroTik setups
cipher AES-256-CBC
ncp-ciphers AES-256-CBC
auth SHA256
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
duplicate-cn
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

# ensure NAT
nft list ruleset | grep -q '10.220.221.0/24' || nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname ens3 ip saddr 10.220.221.0/24 masquerade
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1 >/dev/null

systemctl restart openvpn25-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn25-tcp

PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-connect
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
mkdir -p "$OUT"
for cname in user1 user2; do
cat > "$OUT/${cname}-connect.ovpn" <<EOC
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
auth SHA256
cipher AES-256-CBC
data-ciphers AES-256-CBC
verb 3
ignore-unknown-option disable-dco
disable-dco
compat-mode 2.5.0
route-delay 2
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
cat > "$OUT/${cname}-tap.ovpn" <<EOC
client
dev tun
dev-node "OpenVPN TAP-Windows6"
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
resolv-retry infinite
nobind
persist-key
persist-tun
remote-cert-tls server
auth SHA256
cipher AES-256-CBC
data-ciphers AES-256-CBC
verb 4
disable-dco
compat-mode 2.5.0
route-method exe
route-delay 2
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
ls -la "$OUT"
echo DONE

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from pathlib import Path
import sys
import paramiko

HOST = "5.188.18.103"
PASSWORD = "W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi"
DESKTOP = Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")

REMOTE = r"""#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Prefer OpenVPN that matches modern clients if available
OVPN25=/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn
OVPN27=$(command -v openvpn || true)
ls -la /usr/local/openvpn-*/sbin/openvpn /usr/sbin/openvpn 2>/dev/null || true
$OVPN25 --version 2>&1 | head -1

# Server: block ipv6, explicit topology, allow client IP
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.220.221.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp.txt
keepalive 10 60
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
duplicate-cn
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
push "block-ipv6"
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp block-local"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

# Ensure NAT + forward
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1 >/dev/null
nft list ruleset | grep -q '10.220.221.0/24' || nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "ens3" ip saddr 10.220.221.0/24 masquerade

: > /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log
systemctl restart openvpn25-tcp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn25-tcp
# confirm unit binary
systemctl cat openvpn25-tcp | head -20

PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-connect
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
mkdir -p "$OUT"
for cname in user1 user2; do
cat > "$OUT/${cname}-connect.ovpn" <<EOC
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
persist-tun
persist-key
verb 3
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth none
pull
remote-cert-tls server
ignore-unknown-option disable-dco
ignore-unknown-option block-ipv6
disable-dco
block-ipv6
compat-mode 2.5.0
route-delay 2
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
pull-filter ignore "dhcp-option DNS"
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
cat > "$OUT/${cname}-tap.ovpn" <<EOC
client
dev tun
dev-node "OpenVPN TAP-Windows6"
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
persist-tun
persist-key
verb 4
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth none
pull
remote-cert-tls server
disable-dco
block-ipv6
compat-mode 2.5.0
ip-win32 netsh
route-method exe
route-delay 2
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
ls -la "$OUT"
echo DONE
"""

def main() -> None:
    c = paramiko.SSHClient()
    c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
    c.connect(HOST, username="root", password=PASSWORD, timeout=25, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)
    sftp = c.open_sftp()
    with sftp.file("/root/_fix_ipv6.py.sh", "w") as f:
        f.write(REMOTE)
    sftp.close()
    _, o, e = c.exec_command("bash /root/_fix_ipv6.py.sh")
    sys.stdout.write(o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
    err = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    if err:
        sys.stdout.write("STDERR:" + err)
    sftp = c.open_sftp()
    for n in ("user1-connect.ovpn", "user2-connect.ovpn", "user1-tap.ovpn", "user2-tap.ovpn"):
        sftp.get(f"/root/openvpn-connect/{n}", str(DESKTOP / n))
    sftp.close()
    c.close()
    print("desktop ok")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

input.contents:
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from pathlib import Path
import sys
import paramiko

HOST = "5.188.18.103"
PASSWORD = "W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi"
DESKTOP = Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")

REMOTE = r"""#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-connect
mkdir -p "$OUT" /var/log/openvpn

# Unit -> apt OpenVPN 2.6
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn25-tcp.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=OpenVPN 2.6 TCP for Connect
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target
[Service]
Type=notify
PIDFile=/run/openvpn25-tcp.pid
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/openvpn --status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp-status.log --status-version 2 --writepid /run/openvpn25-tcp.pid --config /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp.conf
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

push "block-ipv6"

tail -15 /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp.log

for cname in user1 user2; do
cat > "$OUT/${cname}-connect.ovpn" <<EOC
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
persist-tun
persist-key
verb 3
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
pull
remote-cert-tls server

ignore-unknown-option block-ipv6
disable-dco
block-ipv6

route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway

<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
cat > "$OUT/${cname}-tap.ovpn" <<EOC
client
dev tun
dev-node "OpenVPN TAP-Windows6"
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
persist-tun
persist-key
verb 4
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
pull
remote-cert-tls server
disable-dco
block-ipv6
ip-win32 netsh
route-method exe
route-delay 2
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
ls -la "$OUT"
echo DONE
"""

def main() -> None:
    c = paramiko.SSHClient()
    c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
    c.connect(HOST, username="root", password=PASSWORD, timeout=25, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)
    sftp = c.open_sftp()
    with sftp.file("/root/_ovpn26.sh", "w") as f:
        f.write(REMOTE)
    sftp.close()
    _, o, e = c.exec_command("bash /root/_ovpn26.sh")
    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    print(o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
    err = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    if err:
        print("STDERR:", err)
    sftp = c.open_sftp()
    for n in ("user1-connect.ovpn", "user2-connect.ovpn", "user1-tap.ovpn", "user2-tap.ovpn"):
        sftp.get(f"/root/openvpn-connect/{n}", str(DESKTOP / n))
    sftp.close()
    c.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from pathlib import Path
import sys
import paramiko

HOST = "5.188.18.103"
PASSWORD = "W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi"
DESKTOP = Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")

REMOTE = r"""#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-connect
mkdir -p "$OUT" /var/log/openvpn

# UDP on 1194 + TCP backup on 4434 (avoid MikroTik OpenVPN TCP helper on 1194)
cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-udp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.220.221.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-udp.txt
keepalive 10 60
compat-mode 2.5.0
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
duplicate-cn
disable-dco
explicit-exit-notify 1
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-udp-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-udp.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
push "block-ipv6"
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp4434.conf <<'EOF'
port 4434
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.220.222.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp4434.txt
keepalive 10 60
compat-mode 2.5.0
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
data-ciphers-fallback AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
duplicate-cn
disable-dco
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp4434-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp4434.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
push "block-ipv6"
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-udp.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=OpenVPN UDP 1194
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target
[Service]
Type=notify
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/openvpn --config /etc/openvpn/server/server-udp.conf --writepid /run/openvpn-udp.pid
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-tcp4434.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=OpenVPN TCP 4434
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target
[Service]
Type=notify
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/openvpn --config /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp4434.conf --writepid /run/openvpn-tcp4434.pid
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Stop old 1194 TCP to free port for UDP
systemctl stop openvpn25-tcp || true
systemctl disable openvpn25-tcp || true

# NAT for both subnets
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1 >/dev/null
nft list ruleset | grep -q '10.220.221.0/24' || nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "ens3" ip saddr 10.220.221.0/24 masquerade || true
nft list ruleset | grep -q '10.220.222.0/24' || nft add rule ip nat POSTROUTING oifname "ens3" ip saddr 10.220.222.0/24 masquerade || true

# firewall open
if command -v ufw >/dev/null; then ufw allow 1194/udp || true; ufw allow 4434/tcp || true; fi
iptables -I INPUT -p udp --dport 1194 -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || true
iptables -I INPUT -p tcp --dport 4434 -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || true

systemctl daemon-reload
: > /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-udp.log
: > /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp4434.log
systemctl enable --now openvpn-udp openvpn-tcp4434
sleep 3
systemctl is-active openvpn-udp openvpn-tcp4434 microsocks
ss -ulnp | grep 1194 || true
ss -tlnp | grep 4434 || true

for cname in user1 user2; do
# Primary for Connect: UDP (bypass TCP helper)
cat > "$OUT/${cname}-connect.ovpn" <<EOC
client
dev tun
proto udp
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 1194
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
persist-tun
persist-key
verb 3
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
pull
remote-cert-tls server
ignore-unknown-option disable-dco
ignore-unknown-option block-ipv6
disable-dco
block-ipv6
explicit-exit-notify 1
compat-mode 2.5.0
route-delay 2
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
pull-filter ignore "dhcp-option DNS"
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
# TCP alt on 4434
cat > "$OUT/${cname}-tcp4434.ovpn" <<EOC
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 4434
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
persist-tun
persist-key
verb 3
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
pull
remote-cert-tls server
ignore-unknown-option disable-dco
ignore-unknown-option block-ipv6
disable-dco
block-ipv6
compat-mode 2.5.0
route-delay 2
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
pull-filter ignore "dhcp-option DNS"
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
ls -la "$OUT"
echo DONE
"""

def main() -> None:
    c = paramiko.SSHClient()
    c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
    c.connect(HOST, username="root", password=PASSWORD, timeout=25, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)
    sftp = c.open_sftp()
    with sftp.file("/root/_udp_ports.sh", "w") as f:
        f.write(REMOTE)
    sftp.close()
    _, o, e = c.exec_command("bash /root/_udp_ports.sh")
    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    print(o.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
    err = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    if err:

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import sys
import paramiko

REMOTE = r"""#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
# Force OpenVPN 2.5.10 binary (no dyn-tls-crypt / protocol-flags)
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-udp.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=OpenVPN 2.5 UDP 1194
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target
[Service]
Type=forking
PIDFile=/run/openvpn-udp.pid
ExecStart=/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --daemon openvpn-udp --writepid /run/openvpn-udp.pid --config /etc/openvpn/server/server-udp.conf
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-udp.conf <<'EOF'
port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.220.221.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-udp.txt
keepalive 10 60
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
duplicate-cn
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-udp-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-udp.log
verb 4
max-clients 5
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

# TCP 4434 also on 2.5
cat > /etc/systemd/system/openvpn-tcp4434.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=OpenVPN 2.5 TCP 4434
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target
[Service]
Type=forking
PIDFile=/run/openvpn-tcp4434.pid
ExecStart=/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --daemon openvpn-tcp4434 --writepid /run/openvpn-tcp4434.pid --config /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp4434.conf
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

cat > /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp4434.conf <<'EOF'
port 4434
proto tcp-server
dev tun
topology subnet
server 10.220.222.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist /var/log/openvpn/ipp-tcp4434.txt
keepalive 10 60
cipher AES-256-GCM
ncp-ciphers AES-256-GCM
auth none
ca /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt
cert /etc/openvpn/server/server.crt
key /etc/openvpn/server/server.key
dh /etc/openvpn/server/dh.pem
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-version-max 1.2
remote-cert-tls client
tun-mtu 1500
persist-key
persist-tun
duplicate-cn
status /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp4434-status.log
status-version 2
log-append /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-tcp4434.log
verb 3
max-clients 5
push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"
push "route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
push "route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway"
EOF

: > /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-udp.log
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl restart openvpn-udp openvpn-tcp4434
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn-udp openvpn-tcp4434
/usr/local/openvpn-2.5/sbin/openvpn --version | head -1
tail -10 /var/log/openvpn/openvpn-udp.log
echo READY
"""

c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect("5.188.18.103", username="root", password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi", timeout=20, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)

input.contents:
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from pathlib import Path
import sys
import paramiko

DESKTOP = Path(r"E:\Data\Users\cherkasov.n\Desktop")
REMOTE = r"""#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
PKI=/etc/openvpn/easy-rsa-rsa/pki
OUT=/root/openvpn-connect
PUBLIC_IP=5.188.18.103
mkdir -p "$OUT"

# Ensure TCP 4434 is up (2.5 binary) + keep UDP as backup
systemctl restart openvpn-tcp4434 openvpn-udp
sleep 2
systemctl is-active openvpn-tcp4434 openvpn-udp microsocks
ss -tlnp | grep 4434
ss -ulnp | grep 1194

for cname in user1 user2; do
# PRIMARY for Connect: TCP 4434
cat > "$OUT/${cname}-connect.ovpn" <<EOC
client
dev tun
proto tcp-client
remote ${PUBLIC_IP} 4434
tun-mtu 1500
tls-client
nobind
persist-tun
persist-key
verb 3
cipher AES-256-GCM
auth none
pull
remote-cert-tls server
route ${PUBLIC_IP} 255.255.255.255 net_gateway
route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.10.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 192.168.20.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
route 10.220.220.0 255.255.255.0 net_gateway
<ca>
$(cat "$PKI/ca.crt")
</ca>
<cert>
$(openssl x509 -in "$PKI/issued/${cname}.crt")
</cert>
<key>
$(cat "$PKI/private/${cname}.key")
</key>
EOC
done
# Also write GCM server push check for 4434
grep -E '^(port|proto|cipher|auth|push)' /etc/openvpn/server/server-tcp4434.conf
ls -la "$OUT"/*-connect.ovpn
echo DONE
"""

c = paramiko.SSHClient()
c.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
c.connect("5.188.18.103", username="root", password="W9yYgPwJ3u69x9Vi", timeout=20, allow_agent=False, look_for_keys=False)
sftp = c.open_sftp()
with sftp.file("/root/_tcp4434_primary.sh", "w") as f:
    f.write(REMOTE)
sftp.close()
_, o, e = c.exec_command("bash /root/_tcp4434_primary.sh")
sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
print(o.read().decode())
print(e.read().decode())
sftp = c.open_sftp()
for n in ("user1-connect.ovpn", "user2-connect.ovpn"):
    sftp.get(f"/root/openvpn-connect/{n}", str(DESKTOP / n))
sftp.close()
c.close()

(DESKTOP / "CONNECT-читай.txt").write_text(
    "OpenVPN Connect\n"
    "===============\n"
    "1. Exit Connect из трея\n"
    "2. Удали старый профиль\n"
    "3. Import user1-connect.ovpn\n"
    "4. Connect\n"
    "\n"
    "Сервер: TCP 5.188.18.103:4434\n"
    "Проверка: https://api.ipify.org = 5.188.18.103\n",
    encoding="utf-8",
)
print("ok")
